123 Portland Street Trust by which name are designated the Trustee or Trustees for the time being, under an Agreement and Declaration of Trust dated May Ist, 1918, and recorded with Suffolk Deeds, Book 4090, page 401, for value received, promises to pay at the office of the State Street Trust Company (hereinafter with its successors in trust, called the Trust Company,) in the City of Boston, to the bearer, on the first day of May, 1938, in accordance with the terms of an Indenture of First Mortgage dated May 1, 1918, between Albert J. Lovett and Roger Ernst, as Trustees under said Agreement and Declaration of Trust, and said Trust Company, the sum of........ dollars ($......) in gold coin of the United States of America, of the present standard of weight and fineness; and also promises to pay at said office of the Trust Company interest on said sum from the first day of May, 1918, at the rate of six per cent. (6%) per annum, payable in like gold coin, semi-annually, on the first days of November and May in each year, on presentation and surrender of the interest coupons hereunder annexed as they severally become due, both principal and interest being payable without any deduction for any present or future tax or taxes which the Trust or the Trust Company may be authorized or required to pay thereon or to retain therefrom under any present or future law or requirement of the United States of America or of any State, county, municipality, or other governmental subdivision thereof, excepting transfer, succession and inheritance taxes, and Federal Income taxes in excess of four per cent. (4%). This bond is one of an issue of coupon bonds limited to an aggregate principal amount of two hundred and seventy-five thousand dollars ($275,000), all issued and to be issued under, and all equally secured by the said Indenture of First Mortgage, dated the first day of May 1918, made and executed by said Albert J. Lovett and Roger Ernst, Trustees as aforesaid, to the State Street Trust Company, as Trustee, and its successors in the trust, to which Indenture of First Mortgage reference is hereby made for a statement of the property mortgaged, the nature and extent of the security, the rights of the bondholders in said security and the terms and conditions upon which said bonds are issued and secured. The bonds are of the denominations of Five Hundred

(500) Dollars and one thousand (1000) dollars each, all otherwise of like tenor and date, and said five hundred dollar bonds are to be numbered from D-1 upwards, and said one thousand dollar bonds are to be numbered from M-1 upwards. This bond may be called and redeemed at a premium or five per cent. (5%) and accrued interest on the principal thereof, on any interest date, after thirty days’ previous notice, in the manner provided in the said mortgage. The agreements herein contained on the part of the said 123 Portland Street Trust are made subject to the provisions of the Agreement and Declaration of Trust hereinbefore mentioned, and the holder of this bond by his acceptance hereof agrees that neither any Trustee nor any shareholder under the said Agreement and Declaration of Trust shall be held to any personal liability under or by reason of any agreement herein contained, but that such holder shall have recourse solely to the property held in trust under said Agreement and Declaration of Trust for the payment or enforcement of any claim hereunder. This bond shall not become obligatory for any purpose until the certificate hereon shall be signed by the Trust Company as Trustee under said mortgage. Now THEREFORE This Indenture Witnesseth:—Albert J. Lovett and Roger Ernst, as they are Trustees as aforesaid, parties of the first part, for and in consideration of the purchase and acceptance of the bonds hereby secured, by the holders hereof, and of the sum of one dollar and other valuable considerations to them paid by the Trust Company at or before the ensealing and delivery of these presents, the receipt whereof is hereby acknowledged, have given, granted, bargained, sold, released, conveyed, assigned, transferred and set over and by these presents, do hereby give, grant, bargain, sell, release, convey, assign, transfer and set over unto the said Trust Company, Trustee, and its successor or successors in said trust the following described premises, to wit:—

A certain parcel of land with the buildings thereon situate in Boston in the County of Suffolk in said Commonwealth and bounded and described as follows:

Southwesterly on Portland Street, seventy-eight 68/100 feet; Northwesterly on land of William D. Sohier and Eliot C. Clarke, trustees under the will of William Sohier, late of Beverly, County of Essex, by a line passing through the middle of the brick partition wall eighty-nine 29/100 feet; Southwesterly on the same land by a line passing through a brick partition wall fourteen 3/100 feet; Northwesterly in part on the end of the passageway leading to Traverse Street and in part on land now or formerly of Eugene F. Ladd, Trustee, by a line and passing through the middle of the brick partition wall fifty-one 88/800 feet; Northeasterly on Friend street ninety-two and 63/100 feet, and Southeasterly on land formerly of Crocker now or late of Redding by lines passing through the middle of the brick party wall one hundred forty-one 33/100 feet; containing eleven thousand eight hundred forty-eight square feet. Also the right to use in common with others for all usual

<br>

### --- Page 0563 ---

APPENDIX OF FORMS

purposes the said passageway leading to said Traverse Street. For further description reference is made to a plan by William A. Johnson dated December 30, 1916, to be recorded with Suffolk Deeds. The premises are conveyed with benefit of and subject to the terms of a certain agreement between Joshua Lovett et al, trustees, and Uriel H. Crocker, trustee, dated December 15, 1896, recorded with Suffolk Deeds, lib. 2408, page 449, and subject to a lease from the trustees of the 123 Portland Street Trust to the Portland Street Garage, Inc., executed as of May Ist, 1918, recorded herewith and prior hereto; but all rights of reversion in the above described premises and all benefits which can run with such reversion are intended to be conveyed hereby. To Have AND To Hold THE property hereby conveyed or assigned with all the privileges and appurtenances thereto belonging, and all property which shall become subject to this Indenture, expressly including any building or buildings hereafter erected on said land, unto and to the use of State Street Trust Company, as Trustee, party of the second part, and its successor or successors and assigns; but IN TRUST, nevertheless, for the equal pro rata benefit, security and protection of the persons, firms and corporations who shall, from time to time, hold the bonds certified, lawfully issued and to be issued under this Indenture and secured hereunder, without any discrimination, preference or priority in the time of issue or negotiation thereof, or otherwise, and in trust for enforcing payment of the principal and interest thereof, according to the tenor of said bonds and of this indenture, and upon the trusts, uses and purposes, and subject to the covenants, agreements, purposes and conditions hereinafter set forthwith and declared; that is to say:—

Article I, Execution, Certification AND DELIVERY OF Bonds.

Section 1. The bonds to be issued under and secured by this Indenture, together with the coupons, shall be substantially of the tenor and purport above recited. The bonds shall be of the denominations of Five Hundred Dollars ($500) and One Thousand Dollars ($1000). The bonds shall be dated as of May 1, 1918, and said five hundred dollar bonds shall be numbered consecutively from D-1 upwards, and said one thousand dollar bonds shall be numbered consecutively from M-1 upwards. All bonds issued hereunder shall be executed in the name of the 123 Portland Street Trust by the Trustee or Trustees for the time being under said agreement and Declaration of Trust dated May Ist, 1918 and shall then be delivered to the Trust Company for certification. The Trust Company shall forthwith or from time to time certify and deliver to the 123 Portland Street Trust, upon its order in writing,

<br>

### --- Page 0564 ---

123 PORTLAND STREET TRUST

bonds issued under and secured by this Indenture in such amounts as such order or orders may specify, provided that the aggregate principal amount of all bonds of this issue outstanding shall at no time exceed two hundred and seventy-five thousand dollars ($275,000). In case any Trustee under the said agreement and declaration of trust dated May Ist, 1918, who shall have signed or sealed any of the bonds of this issue shall cease to be such Trustee before the bonds so signed or sealed shall have been actually certified and delivered by the Trust Company, such bonds, nevertheless, may be issued, certified and delivered as though such Trustee who so signed or sealed such bonds had not ceased to be a Trustee under said agreement and declaration of trust. The coupons to be attached shall be authenticated by a facsimile of the signatures of the present Trustees or of any future Trustee under said agreement and declaration of trust, and the 123 Portland Street Trust may adopt and use for that purpose the facsimile signature of any person who shall have been such Trustee, notwithstanding the fact that he may have ceased to be such Trustee at the time when such bonds shall be actually certified and delivered. Before certifying or delivering any coupon bond, all coupons thereon then matured shall be cut off and cancelled and on its written demand delivered to the 123 Portland Street Trust. Only such bonds as shall bear thereon a certificate substantially in the form hereinbefore recited, signed on behalf of the Trust Company by its Treasurer or other duly authorized officer, shall be secured by this Indenture or entitled to any lien, right or benefit hereunder, and the fact that such certificate has been placed by the Trust Company upon any such bond, executed by the 123 Portland Street Trust shall be conclusive evidence as against the 123 Portland Street Trust that the bond so certified has been duly issued hereunder. Section 2. Until the bonds to be ultimately issued under this Indenture shall be prepared, the 123 Portland Street Trust may execute, and upon its request the Trust Company shall certify and deliver, in lieu of such permanent bonds, and subject to the same provisions, one or more temporary bonds of any denomination, either registered or negotiable by delivery, without coupons. Upon the surrender to, and cancellation by, the Trust Company of said temporary bond or bonds, or any of them, the Trust Company shall certify permanent bonds to the face value of such temporary bond or bonds so surrendered and cancelled. Said temporary bond or bonds, until so exchanged, shall be secured by and entitled to the benefit of provisions of this Indenture to the same extent as the bonds to be ultimately issued hereunder. In case any bond issued hereunder shall become mutilated, destroyed or lost, the 123 Portland Street Trust, in its discretion, may execute, and the Trust Company, at

<br>

### --- Page 0565 ---

APPENDIX OF FORMS

its request, shall certify and deliver a new bond of like tenor and date (including in the case of a coupon bond, the coupons) and bearing the same serial number as the bond so mutilated, destroyed or lost. In case of mutilation the applicant for such bond or coupons shall surrender the mutilated bond or coupons for cancellation. In case of destruction or loss the applicant for the substituted bond or coupons shall furnish to the 123 Portland Street Trust and the Trust Company satisfactory evidence of the destruction or loss of such bond, and also such security or indemnity as may be required by the 123 Portland Street Trust and the Trust Company.

ARTICLE II. CALL AND PAYMENT OF BONDS.

The 123 Portland Street Trust may, at any time or times prior to their maturity, call all or any part of the outstanding bonds secured hereby for payment on any interest date, at their face value and interest accrued to the date of payment, together with five per cent. (5%) premium on the face of said bonds, by publishing notices of such call once in each week for three successive weeks in one or more newspapers published in Boston, Massachusetts, the first publication to be not less than thirty (30) days prior to the date fixed in the call for such payment, and by depositing with the Trust Company on or before the date fixed in the call for such payment a sum of money sufficient therefor, together with the reasonable compensation, expenses and disbursements of the Trust Company. The moneys so deposited shall be held by the Trust Company to the credit and for the payment of the bonds so called, and, if so deposited, interest on all bonds so called shall cease from and after the date fixed in the call for payment. In case the 123 Portland Street Trust shall at any time call, for payment, less than the whole number of bonds at such time outstanding, it shall draw by lot the bonds so to be called, and the numbers of the bonds so drawn shall be stated in the notice to be published in the manner above provided. On the deposit with the Trust Company of the amount necessary so to redeem all bonds hereby secured, then outstanding, together with proof satisfactory to the Trust Company of the due publication of the notice required by this Article of the call of all such bonds, and the payment to the Trust Company of its reasonable compensation, expenses and disbursements, then unpaid, the Trust Company shall cancel and discharge this indenture, and assign and redeliver the mortgaged property, including all moneys, held by it under the provisions hereof (not including, however, any moneys deposited as aforesaid for the purposes of redemption of said bonds) to the 123 Portland Street Trust, and shall execute all such reconveyances or

<br>

### --- Page 0566 ---

123 PORTLAND STREET TRUST

releases of the mortgaged property as may be necessary or desirable to free it from the lien hereof; and all bonds redeemed and paid shall forthwith be cancelled.

ARTICLE III. Particular COVENANTS OF 123 PORTLAND Street Trust.

Section 1. The 123 Portland Street Trust covenants that it shall and will promptly pay, or cause to be paid, the principal and interest of the bonds hereby secured, as and when the same shall become due and payable, according to the terms thereof and hereof, subject, however, to all the provisions of Article VII hereof, without any deduction for any present or future tax or taxes which the Trust or the Trust Company may be authorized or required to pay thereon or to retain therefrom under any present or future law or requirement of the United States of America or of any State, county, municipality, or other governmental subdivision thereof, excepting transfer, succession and inheritance taxes and Federal Income Taxes in excess of four per cent. (4%). As the coupons annexed to said bonds are paid in full, they shall be cancelled.

Section 2. The 123 Portland Street Trust covenants, subject as aforesaid, to the provisions of Article VII, that it is lawfully seized and possessed of all the aforesaid mortgaged premises, that they are free and clear from all encumbrances having priority over these presents except as aforesaid, that it has good right and lawful authority to mortgage the same as herein provided, and that it will warrant and defend the interest of the Trust Company therein under this mortgage against the lawful claims of all persons except as aforesaid.

Section 3. The 123 Portland Street Trust covenants, subject as aforesaid, that it will, upon reasonable request of the Trust Company, execute, acknowledge and deliver any and all mortgages supplemental or additional hereto and such further instruments and do such further acts as may be necessary or proper to carry out more effectually the purposes of this indenture, or to transfer to and confirm in the Trust Company the estate, property, or funds held in trust hereunder, and for the better assuring to and confirming in the Trust Company all and singular the premises and property hereby conveyed or assigned or intended so to be.

Section 4. The 123 Portland Street Trust covenants and agrees, subject as aforesaid, to cause this mortgage and every additional instrument which shall be executed pursuant to Section 3 of this Article to be recorded and filed in such manner and in such places as may be required to preserve and protect the security of the bondholders, the superior lien hereof on the mortgaged estate and the rights and remedies of the Trust Company; and the Trust Company shall in no wise be liable for any failure or omission in

<br>

### --- Page 0567 ---

APPENDIX OF FORMS

this regard. Section 5. The 123 Portland Street Trust covenants, subject as aforesaid, that it will pay all valid taxes, assessments and governmental charges assessed against the property conveyed hereby, so that the lien of this mortgage shall be maintained unimpaired at all times during the continuance hereof; and that it will also pay all valid taxes, assessments and governmental charges which may be assessed upon the interest of the Trust Company in the mortgaged property, whether the same be assessed to the 123 Portland Street Trust or to the Trust Company; provided, nevertheless, that the 123 Portland Street Trust shall have the right to contest any such tax, assessment or charge, and pending such contest may delay or defer payment thereof. Section 6. The 123 Portland Street Trust covenants, subject as aforesaid, that it will keep all its property which is at any one time covered by this mortgage (and which is of a character usually insured) insured against loss or damage by fire to an amount equal to at least eighty per cent. (80%) of the insurable value of said property, and against loss or damage by boiler explosion and other risks against which similar properties are usually insured, from time to time, by reputable insurance companies, and shall cause all insurance policies upon the mortgaged property to provide that the loss shall be made payable to the Trust Company, as its interest may appear.

In case the property covered by the lien of this indenture is not so insured, the Trustee may cause insurance to be placed thereon, and in such event the Trustee shall, upon the written request of the holders of 1/10 part in amount of the bonds then outstanding, specifying the property to be insured and the nature and amount of insurance, and upon being indemnified to its reasonable satisfaction cause insurance to be placed on the property specified in such request in the amount therein required, and, in every such case, the 123 Portland Street Trust shall repay to the Trustee upon demand the amount of the premiums expended by it necessary to make good the covenant of the 123 Portland Street Trust contained above in this section, together with interest thereon at the rate of six (6%) per centum per annum from the time of its expenditure by the Trustee. The amount of such expenditure so to be repaid by the 123 Portland Street Trust, with interest until repaid to the Trustee, shall constitute a lien on the property covered by these presents, and in case of default shall be repaid to the Trustee before any payments are made on any bonds or coupons as herein provided. The Trustee shall not be responsible for the insuring of property or for the renewal of such insurance or for the amount of insurance, except when requested and indemnified as aforesaid, and then only for neglect to comply with such request. All such policies shall remain in the possession of the Trust Company.

<br>

### --- Page 0568 ---

123 PORTLAND STREET TRUST

company. The proceeds of any insurance of whatever nature, on any part of the mortgaged property, which may be received by the Trust Company, may be applied by the 123 Portland Street Trust to restoring, repairing, replacing or building substitutes for the property injured or destroyed, or otherwise improving or building upon the mortgaged property, all of which substitutes, improvements or buildings shall forthwith be subjected to the lien hereof; and the Trust Company shall from time to time as the work progresses pay over such proceeds to the 123 Portland Street Trust upon its request and upon receipt from it of proper vouchers showing that such substitutes, improvements or buildings have been constructed, since the date of the loss on account of which such proceeds were received, to the value of the payment or payments so requested, and that no other payments have been made by the Trust Company to the 123 Portland Street Trust on account thereof. The Trust Company shall accept as conclusive evidence of the advisability of approving the application of the proceeds of insurance to any of the aforesaid purposes the certificate of the 123 Portland Street Trust. The Trust Company shall hold such proceeds until so paid over as security for the payment of the bonds and coupons secured hereby, or upon request in writing of the 123 Portland Street Trust shall use said proceeds for the call and payment of bonds in accordance with the provisions of Article II hereof. Section 7. The 123 Portland Street Trust covenants, subject as aforesaid, that it will after the completion of the new building, which it is authorized by Article IV hereof to erect upon the mortgaged premises, and during the continuance hereof, keep all buildings on the mortgaged premises in good and substantial repair; that it will not during such period commit strip or waste in respect to such buildings and will not pull down or remove the same, or any part thereof, unless such pulling down or removal shall be rendered necessary by any of the said premises being worn out or injured, in which case it shall replace the premises or property worn out or injured by other property of at least equal value. Section 8. The 123 Portland Street Trust covenants that it will at any and all times upon the written request of the Trustee (a) Permit the Trustee, by its agents and attorneys, to examine all the books of account, records, reports and other papers of the trust, and to take copies and extracts therefrom. (b) Furnish to the Trustee a detailed and true balance sheet showing the financial condition of the trust and a full and detailed statement of its earnings and expenses for a period of at least twelve months prior to the date of such request.

<br>

### --- Page 0569 ---

APPENDIX OF FORMS

Article IV. Possession Until Default.

Until default shall be made in the payment of the principal or interest of the bonds or any of them issued hereunder, or in the performance by the 123 Portland Street Trust of any of the covenants herein contained, and such default shall have continued beyond the period of grace, if any, herein provided in respect thereof, the 123 Portland Street Trust shall be suffered and permitted to possess, use, maintain and enjoy all the property and rights of every kind conveyed by this mortgage, and every part thereof, with the appurtenances thereto, and to take and use the income, rents, issues and profits thereof, with power freely and without let or hindrance on the part of the Trust Company or of the bondholders to erect thereon a new building of such design as it shall deem best and thereafter to repair or improve any part of the mortgaged property, and generally to possess and use the mortgaged estate to the same extent and in the same manner as if this indenture had not been made, provided that the security hereof shall not thereby, after the completion of such new building, be in any wise reduced or impaired. The 123 Portland Street Trust may also, until default shall have been made and continued as aforesaid, from time to time lease, free from all encumbrances and trusts hereof, any part of the property at any time subject to the lien hereof for any term not exceeding twenty (20) years, to take effect in possession within one (1) year from the date of the lease, or in case of the erection of any new building, to take effect upon the completion thereof, or in case of the extension of an existing lease to take effect upon the expiration thereof, provided that there be reserved in every such lease a fair and reasonable rent or rents, the judgment of the 123 Portland Street Trust as to the fairness and reasonableness of any such rent or rents, however, to be conclusive; and provided, further, that there be contained in every such lease a condition of re-entry for nonpayment within a reasonable time, to be therein specified, of the rent or rents thereby reserved, or the breach of any covenant or condition therein contained, and on the part of the lessee or lessees, his or their executors, administrators, and assigns, or its successors and assigns, to be observed and performed, and as soon as the lessee or lessees do execute a counterpart or duplicate thereof, and do thereby covenant for the due payment of the rent or rents thereby reserved. Nothing in this paragraph contained shall be construed to apply to the existing lease of the trust premises to Portland Street Garage, Incorporated, to which lease this mortgage is subject, or to the renewal of said lease for a further period of ten years as therein contemplated.

<br>

### --- Page 0570 ---

423 PORTLAND STREET TRUST

ARTICLE V. REMEDIES OF TRUSTEE AND BONDHOLDERS UPON
DEFAULT.

Section 1. In case default shall be made in the payment of any instalment of interest on any of the bonds hereby secured, according to the terms thereof and hereof, and said default shall have continued for a period of six (6) months thereafter, then and in such case the Trust Company may, and, if the holders of twenty five per cent. (25%) in amount of the bonds then outstanding in writing so request, the Trust Company, by notice in writing addressed and mailed to the 123 Portland Street Trust at its office in Boston, if any, or to the office of either of the then trustees, shall declare the principal of all the said bonds immediately due and payable, and thereupon the same shall forthwith become and be due and payable. This provision, however, is subject to the condition that if at any time after the principal of said bonds shall have been so declared due and payable, and before any sale of the mortgaged premises shall have been made, all arrears of interest upon all the said bonds, with interest at the rate of six per cent. (6%) per annum on overdue instalments of interest and all other amounts in respect of which the 123 Portland Street Trust shall then be in default under this mortgage other than the principal of the said bonds, shall, together with the expenses of the Trust Company, either be paid by the 123 Portland Street Trust or be collected out of the mortgaged premises, then and in every such case the holders of a majority in amount of the bonds then outstanding may, by written notice to the Trust Company, authorize the Trust Company to waive such default and its consequences, and the Trust Company receiving such notice shall waive such default and its consequences by written notice to the 123 Portland Street Trust; but no such waiver shall extend to or affect any subsequent default or impair any rights consequent thereon. In case the Trust Company shall have proceeded to enforce any right under this mortgage by foreclosure, entry or otherwise, and such proceedings shall have been discontinued or abandoned because of such waiver or for any other reason, or shall have been determined adversely to the Trust Company, then and in every such case the 123 Portland Street Trust and the Trust Company shall be restored to their former position and rights hereunder with respect to the mortgaged premises, and all rights, remedies and powers of the Trust Company shall continue as though no such proceedings had been taken. Anything to the contrary herein contained notwithstanding, in the event of the sale of the mortgaged premises under the power or trust for sale hereinafter contained, or pursuant

<br>

### --- Page 0571 ---

APPENDIX OF FORMS

to judicial proceedings, the principal of all the bonds secured hereby shall become and be forthwith due and payable. Section 2. In case default shall be made by the 123 Portland Street Trust in the payment of the principal of any of the bonds hereby secured when the same shall become due and payable, whether at maturity or under any provision hereof; or in case default shall be made by the 123 Portland Street Trust in the payment of any instalment of interest on any of said bonds, according to the terms thereof and hereof, which shall continue for a period of six (6) months; or in case default shall be made in the due observance or performance of any of the other covenants or agreements herein required to be kept or performed by the 123 Portland Street Trust, which last mentioned default shall continue for the period of six (6) months after written notice thereof from the Trust Company to the 123 Portland Street Trust, the Trust Company may in its discretion, with or without entry, sell and dispose of the mortgaged premises and all property covered by the terms of this mortgage, at public auction in the city of Boston, Massachusetts, at such time as the Trust Company may determine; but the Trust Company shall not exercise the power or trust for sale herein contained until it shall have first given public notice of such sale and the time and place thereof by advertisement in one daily newspaper published in the city of Boston, Massachusetts, not less than once a week for four consecutive weeks, and in such other manner, if any, as may be required by law. The Trust Company shall have the right to adjourn any such sale or sales by announcement at the time and place appointed for such sale or for such adjourned sale or sales; and without further notice or publication such sale may be made at the time and place to which the same may be so adjourned, and shall have the right in its discretion to sell the mortgaged property in one or several parcels, and to make successive sales of any part thereof remaining undisposed of at any such sale or sales. Upon the completion of any sale or sales under this mortgage the Trust Company is hereby further empowered and authorized, either in its own name or the name of the 123 Portland Street Trust, to make, execute, and deliver to the accepted purchaser or purchasers a good and sufficient deed or good and sufficient deeds of conveyance of the property so sold; and the Trust Company is hereby irrevocably appointed the true and lawful attorney of the 123 Portland Street Trust, in its name and stead to make all necessary transfers of the property thus sold, and for that purpose the Trust Company may execute all necessary acts of assignment and transfer, the 123 Portland Street Trust hereby ratifying and confirming all that its said attorney may lawfully do by virtue hereof. Any such sale or sales made under or by virtue of this mortgage, whether under the

<br>

### --- Page 0572 ---

123 PORTLAND STREET TRUST

power of sale hereby granted and conferred or under or by virtue of judicial proceedings, shall operate to divest all right, title, interest, claim and demand whatsoever, either at law or in equity, of the 123 Portland Street Trust of, in and to the property so sold, and shall be a perpetual bar, both at law and in equity, against the 123 Portland Street Trust and its successors and assigns, and against any and all persons claiming the property sold, or any part thereof, from, through or under the 123 Portland Street Trust, its successors and assigns.

Section 3. In case default shall be made by the 123 Portland Street Trust in the payment of the principal of any of the bonds hereby secured, when the same shall become due and payable, whether at maturity or under any provision hereof; or in case default shall be made by the 123 Portland Street Trust in the payment of any instalment of interest on any of said bonds, according to the terms thereof and hereof, which shall continue for the period of six (6) months; or in case default shall be made in the due observance or performance of any of the other covenants or agreements herein required to be kept or performed by the 123 Portland Street Trust, which shall continue for the period of six (6) months after written notice thereof from the Trust Company to the 123 Portland Street Trust, then the Trust Company in its discretion may, either after entry as hereinbefore provided or without such entry, proceed by suit or suits at law or in equity, or by any other appropriate remedy, as the Trust Company may be advised, to enforce payment of the said bonds and to foreclose this mortgage and to sell the mortgaged premises, and all property covered by the terms of this mortgage, under the judgment or decree of a court or courts of competent jurisdiction. In case the Trust Company shall proceed, by suits at law or in equity, after default occurring and continuing as above provided, the Trust Company shall be entitled to have the premises and property hereby granted and conveyed, or intended so to be, sold at judicial sale under the order of a court or courts of competent jurisdiction, for or toward the satisfaction of the principal or interest or both due and owing, upon the bonds hereby secured, and for the enforcement of the rights, liens and securities of the Trust Company and the bondholders, and shall be entitled as a matter of substantial right to a receivership of the mortgaged premises and property, and of the tolls, rents, earnings, revenues, issues, profits and income thereof. The 123 Portland Street Trust will not impede or delay, or attempt to impede or delay, the Trust Company or any of the holders of the bonds hereby secured in lawfully exercising any right or power conferred upon it or them by this mortgage, or otherwise lawfully enforcing their respective rights hereunder.

Section 4. Upon request in writing signed by the holders of not less than twenty

<br>

### --- Page 0573 ---

APPENDIX OF FORMS

five per cent. (25%) in amount of the bonds hereby secured then outstanding, and upon being reasonably indemnified against costs, liabilities, and expenses which may be incurred by acting in accordance with such request, it shall be the duty of the Trust Company in any case of default which shall occur and shall have continued as specified in Section 3 hereof, to take all needful steps for the protection and enforcement of the rights hereby secured to it as Trustee for the holders of said bonds and coupons, and to exercise the powers of entry or sale herein conferred, or both, or to take proper judicial proceedings by action, suit or otherwise as the Trust Company, being advised by counsel learned in the law, shall deem most expedient for the interests of the bondholders. The Trust Company in all cases, whether acting on its own motion or upon request as aforesaid, shall have the right to determine which of the remedies provided herein, or which of those as may be provided by law, it shall adopt, and its discretion in this respect shall be conclusive and binding on all the bondholders.

Section 5. The purchase money, proceeds and avails of any sale hereunder, whether made under the power of sale hereby granted or otherwise, together with any other sums which then may be held by the Trust Company under any of the provisions of this mortgage, as part of the Trust estate or the proceeds hereof, shall be applied as follows: First. To the payment of the costs and expenses of said sale, including a reasonable compensation to the Trust Company, its agents, attorneys and counsel, and all expenses, liabilities and advances made or incurred by the Trust Company, and all taxes and assessments and other charges prior to the lien of these presents, except so far as the sale shall have been made subject thereto. Second. To the payment of the whole amount then owing or unpaid upon the bonds hereby secured for principal and interest, with interest on overdue instalments of interest at the rate of six per centum (6%) per annum, and, in case such proceeds shall be insufficient to pay in full the whole amount so due and unpaid upon the said bonds, then to the payment of said principal and interest, without preference or priority of principal over interest or of interest over principal, or of any instalment of interest over any other instalment of interest, ratably to the aggregate amount of such principal and accrued and unpaid interest. Third. To the payment of any surplus to the Portland Street Trust, or its assigns, or to whomsoever may be lawfully entitled to receive the same.

Section 6. Upon any sale hereunder, whether made under the power of sale hereby granted or otherwise, the receipt of the Trust Company or of the officer making such sale shall be a sufficient discharge to the purchaser or purchasers at such sale for his or their purchase money, and

<br>

### --- Page 0574 ---

such purchaser or purchasers shall not be obliged to see to the application of such purchase money, and shall not be in any wise answerable for any loss or misapplication or non-application thereof by the Trust Company or such officer. Section 7. Upon any such sale as aforesaid any holder or holders of any of the bonds hereby secured may bid and become the purchaser or purchasers thereat, and may hold and enjoy the property purchased without accountability to any other holder; and any purchaser or purchasers, upon presenting any of said bonds or interest coupons, shall be entitled to be credited on account of the purchase price with a sum which would, upon a proper distribution and accounting of the proceeds of sale, be equal to the distributive share, payable out of such proceeds, to the holder of the bonds or coupons so presented, which amount so credited shall be stamped or endorsed on such bonds or coupons as paid thereon. Section 8. Except as herein expressly provided to the contrary, no remedy herein conferred upon or reserved to the Trust Company or to the holders of bonds hereby secured is intended to be exclusive of any other remedy, but each and every such remedy shall be cumulative and shall be in addition to any other remedy given hereunder, or now or hereafter existing at law, or in equity or by statute. Section 9. No delay or omission of the Trust Company or of any holder of bonds hereby secured to exercise any right or power accruing upon any default, continuing as aforesaid, shall impair any such right or power, or shall be construed to be a waiver of any such default or an acquiescence therein.

Article VI. Coupons Pledged After Maturity.

No coupons belonging to any of the bonds hereby secured, which in any way on or after maturity shall be transferred or pledged separate and apart from its bonds, shall be entitled to any benefit of or from this mortgage, except after prior full payment of the principal of all bonds issued hereunder and of all coupons and interest obligations not so transferred or pledged.

Article VII. Waiver or Liability of Trustees and Shareholders.

This indenture, and the bonds herein referred to, are executed by the 123 Portland Street Trust subject to the provisions of the agreement and declaration of trust dated May 1st, 1918, hereinbefore referred to, and the Trust Company by its acceptance of this indenture as Trustee and the holders of bonds issued hereunder by their acceptance thereof agree that neither said Albert J. Lovett nor Roger Ernst, nor any Trustee or shareholder of the 123 Portland Street Trust under said agreement and declaration

<br>

### --- Page 0575 ---

of trust dated May Ist, 1918, shall be held to any personal liability whatsoever under or by reason of any or all of the conditions, covenants, promises, and agreements contained in this indenture and the bonds and coupons issued hereunder, and that no claim under or in pursuance of the said bonds and coupons or under or in pursuance of this indenture or the terms and provisions hereof shall be enforcible except against the funds and property of the trust from time to time in the hands of the Trustee or Trustees for the time being under said agreement and declaration of trust dated May Ist, 1918; and no personal liability whatsoever shall attach to or is incurred by said Albert J. Lovett or Roger Ernst or any Trustee or shareholder of said trust because of the incurring of any indebtedness hereby authorized, or under or by reason of any of the obligations, covenants or agreements contained in this indenture, or of any of the bonds or coupons issued hereunder or implied therefrom; and any and all personal liability of every name and nature and every right or claim against said Albert J. Lovett or Roger Ernst or any such Trustee or shareholder, howsoever arising, is hereby expressly released and waived as a condition of and part of the consideration for the execution of this indenture and the issue of the bonds and coupons hereunder; provided, however, that any claim against the 123 Portland Street Trust under said bonds or coupons or under this indenture shall be a charge upon, and shall be enforcible against the property and effects belonging to the 123 Portland Street Trust under said agreement and declaration of trust, at the time of proceedings to enforce the same, and that nothing herein contained shall prevent the Trust Company from obtaining and enforcing such decree against the 123 Portland Street Trust as may be necessary or appropriate to compel the application of the property and effects then belonging to the 123 Portland Street Trust under said agreement and declaration of trust to the performance of the stipulations of this indenture and the bonds issued hereunder, or to prevent the 123 Portland Street Trust from violating any stipulations in this indenture or in said bonds contained.

Article VIII. Concerning the Trustee.

Section 1. The Trust Company shall be entitled to be reimbursed for all proper outlays of whatever sort or nature to be incurred in or about this trust, including counsel fees, and to receive a reasonable compensation for any duties that it may at any time perform in the discharge of the same; and all such fees, commissions, compensation and disbursements shall constitute a lien prior to the bonds hereby secured on the mortgaged property and premises and on all sums in its hands hereunder. It shall be

<br>

### --- Page 0576 ---

123 PORTLAND STREET TRUST

entitled to advice of counsel on all matters concerning the trust, and shall be held only to the exercise of reasonable care and diligence in relation to carrying out the provisions of this instrument. The Trust Company may act by its agents or attorneys, and shall not be responsible for their negligence or wrongdoing; but it shall be held to the exercise of reasonable care in selecting them. The Trust Company shall be answerable only for its own several acts, receipts, neglects and defaults, and not for those of any other or of any succeeding Trustee or Trustees. The Trust Company shall not be bound to recognize any person or party as a holder of any of the bonds hereby secured, nor to take any action at his request unless his bond or bonds are submitted to the Trust Company for inspection, or his ownership thereof is otherwise shown to its satisfaction. Nor shall it, until required to take active measures to enforce the security of foreclosure or otherwise, with due indemnity, be any part of the Trust Company’s duty to keep itself informed or advised as to the performance of any of the 123 Portland Street Trust’s covenants, or the payment or discharge of any taxes, assessments or liens that may be imposed upon the property, real or personal, sought to be affected by this mortgage, or to require the payment or discharge of such taxes, assessments, or liens, nor to see to the placing of insurance upon the mortgaged premises, nor to the amount or sufficiency thereof, but the Trust Company may in its discretion do any and all of these things. The Trust Company shall not have any responsibility as to the validity of this mortgage or of the bonds secured hereby, or as to the execution, acknowledgement or recording hereof, or as to otherwise giving notice of the lien of the mortgage or renewal hereof, or as to the recording or filing of any subsequent or supplemental instrument, or as to the amount or adequacy as security of the property hereby conveyed, or as to the title thereof, or as to the use of any of the said bonds or the application of the proceeds thereof. The recitals herein contained and in the bonds secured hereby are made by the 123 Portland Street Trust solely, and the Trust Company shall not in any wise be accountable for the correctness of the same. The Trust Company shall be protected in acting upon any notice, request, consent, certificate, bond or other paper or document believed by it to be genuine and to be signed by the proper party. The Trust Company shall not be required to take notice or to be deemed to have knowledge of any default of the 123 Portland Street Trust hereunder, and it may conclusively assume that there has been no such default unless and until it shall have been notified specifically in writing of such default by the 123 Portland Street Trust, or by the holders of not less than ten per cent. (10%) in amount of the bonds then out-

<br>

### --- Page 0577 ---

APPENDIX OF FORMS

standing hereunder. Section 2. It is further agreed that the Trust Company may at any time resign the trust by written notice addressed and mailed to the 123 Portland Street Trust, at its principal office in Boston, Massachusetts, or, in case there is no such office, to the office of any Trustee of said 123 Portland Street Trust, and the Trust Company shall so resign at any time in case the holders of a majority of the bonds hereby secured then outstanding shall in writing request such resignation, and such resignation shall take effect upon the appointment of a new Trustee or after sixty (60) days’ notice to the 123 Portland Street Trust. It is agreed that in case of the resignation of the said State Street Trust Company, or any successor to such Trust Company, the new Trustee to be appointed in its stead shall be a Trust Company incorporated under the laws of the Commonwealth of Massachusetts. Any vacancy in the office of Trustee may be filled by any court having jurisdiction, on application of the outgoing Trustee or any holder or holders of the bonds then outstanding. No bond shall be required of any Trustee unless ordered by the court for cause shown. Upon the appointment of any successor to the trust above mentioned to fill any vacancy in the office of Trustee, created by resignation or otherwise, all the interest of the outgoing Trustee shall immediately and without conveyance vest in the new Trustee. But the outgoing Trustee, its successors or assigns, shall, nevertheless, at the request of any person interested, but at the expense of the trust, execute, acknowledge and deliver to the new Trustee such deeds of conveyance or other instruments in writing as may be appropriate to vest or confirm in the new Trustee the mortgaged or trust property.

Article IX. Bondholders’ Rights.

Section 1. No holder of any bond or coupon hereby secured shall have any right to institute any suit, action or proceedings in equity or at law, for the foreclosure of this mortgage, or for the execution of any trust hereof, or for the appointment of a receiver, or for any other remedy hereunder, unless such holder shall have previously given to the Trust Company written notice of such default and of the continuance thereof as hereinbefore provided, nor unless also the holders of twenty-five per cent. (25%) in amount of the bonds then outstanding shall have made written request to the Trust Company and shall have offered reasonable opportunity, either to proceed to exercise the powers hereinbefore granted, or to institute such action, suit or proceeding in its own name; nor unless also they shall have offered to the Trust Company adequate security and indemnity against the costs, expenses and

<br>

### --- Page 0578 ---

123 PORTLAND STREET TRUST

liabilities to be incurred therein or thereby; and such notification,
request and offer of indemnity are hereby declared, in every such
case, at the option of the Trust Company, to be conditions precedent
to the execution of the powers and trusts of this mortgage,
and to any action or cause of action for foreclosure or for the appointment
of a receiver, or for any other remedy hereunder;
it being understood and intended that no one or more holders of
the bonds or coupons hereby secured shall have any right in any
manner whatsoever to affect, disturb or prejudice the lien of this
mortgage by his or their action to enforce any right hereunder,
except in the manner herein provided, and that all proceedings
at law or in equity shall be instituted, had and maintained in the
manner herein provided, and for the equal benefit of all holders of
outstanding bonds and coupons. Section 2. In any proceeding
instituted either by the Trust Company or by the holders of said
bonds for the purpose of exercising any of the rights or powers
granted by this mortgage, it or they shall be under no obligation
to present any of the said bonds in court.

Article X. Requests Upon Truster By Bondholders.

Any request or other instrument which this mortgage may
require or permit to be signed and executed by the bondholders
may be in any number of concurrent instruments of similar
tenor, and may be signed or executed by such bondholders in
person or by attorney appointed in writing. Proof of the execution
of any such request or other instrument, or of a writing appointing
any such agent, or of the holding by any person of the
bonds or coupons appertaining thereto, shall be sufficient for any
purpose of this mortgage if made in the following manner:—(a)
The fact and date of the execution by any person of such request
or other instrument in writing may be proved by the certificate
of any notary public, or other officer, authorized to take acknowledgments
of deeds to be recorded in any State, that the person
signing such request or other instrument acknowledged to him the
execution thereof, or by an affidavit of a witness of such execution;
(b) The amount of bonds transferable by delivery held by any
person executing such request or other instrument as a bondholder,
and the issue numbers thereof held by such person, and the date of
his holding the same, may be proven by a certificate executed by
any trust company, bank, bankers or other depositary wheresoever
situated, if such certificate shall be deemed by the Trust
Company to be satisfactory, showing that at the date therein
mentioned such person had on deposit with such depositary the
bonds described in such certificate. The Trust Company shall
not be bound to recognize any person as a bondholder unless and

<br>

### --- Page 0579 ---

APPENDIX OF FORMS

until his title to the bonds held by him is proved in the manner in this section provided.

Article XI. Recourse Reserved To Parties.

Nothing in this mortgage, or in the bonds hereby secured, expressed or implied, is intended or shall be construed to give any person or corporation, other than the parties hereto and the holders of the said bonds, any legal or equitable right, remedy or claim under or in respect of this mortgage, or under any covenant, condition, or provision herein contained; all the covenants, conditions, and provisions herein being intended to be, and being, for the sole and exclusive benefit of the parties hereto, and of the holders of the bonds and interest obligations appurtenant thereto.

Article XII. Discharge.

These presents shall become void—(a) If the 123 Portland Street Trust shall as herein provided call all bonds outstanding hereunder, and shall pay the Trustee such sum of money as shall, in addition to any other moneys then in the hands of the Trustee and applicable to that purpose, be sufficient to pay all such bonds not delivered to the Trustee, cancelled or for cancellation, including the premium above mentioned and accrued interest to the date fixed in the call for payment; and in such case as of the said date of payment; or (b) If the 123 Portland Street Trust shall well and truly pay and discharge at the maturity thereof the principal and interest of all bonds then outstanding hereunder in the manner therein provided; and in such case as of the date of maturity aforesaid; or (c) If the 123 Portland Street Trust shall at any time before May 1, 1938, pay the Trustee such sum of money as shall in addition to any other moneys then in the hands of the Trustee and applicable to that purpose, be sufficient to pay all the bonds then outstanding hereunder, and interest thereon to maturity, or sufficient to make such payment on all such bonds as the 123 Portland Street Trust shall not have delivered to the Trustee, cancelled or for cancellation; and in such case as of the date of payment to the Trustee aforesaid, or (d) If the 123 Portland Street Trust shall at any time deliver to the Trustee, cancelled, all the bonds and coupons then outstanding hereunder; and in such case forthwith; (e) But only if in each case and every such case the 123 Portland Street Trust shall have paid to the Trustee any and all sums due to the Trustee and such additional sum, if any, as in the opinion of the Trustee is sufficient to cover any other liability of the 123 Portland Street Trust under any of the foregoing provisions of this indenture. And

<br>

### --- Page 0580 ---

123 PORTLAND STREET TRUST

then and in each and every such case, the estate, right, and title of the Trustee hereby created shall utterly cease, and determine; and if the 123 Portland Street Trust shall so request, the Trustee shall, upon such request on or after the dates respectively above set forth, execute to the 123 Portland Street Trust a good and sufficient release and discharge of this indenture and of the lien hereby created and shall restore and surrender possession to the 123 Portland Street Trust of any property of which it shall have taken possession and which it shall not have sold under and by virtue of these presents; but otherwise, and until such payment and performance, these presents shall be and remain in full force and effect.

All the covenants, stipulations, promises and agreements in this indenture contained by the 123 Portland Street Trust shall bind and be binding upon its successors and assigns, whether or not so expressed. In order to facilitate the recording of this indenture, the same may be simultaneously executed in three counterparts, each of which so executed shall be deemed to be an original, and such counterparts together shall constitute but one and the same instrument. The State Street Trust Company, party hereto of the second part, hereby accepts the trusts in this indenture declared and provided, and agrees to perform the same upon the terms and conditions hereinbefore set forth.

In Witness WHEREOF the said ALBERT J. Lovett AND RoGER Ernst, as they are Trustees of the 123 Portland Street Trust, under an agreement and declaration of Trust dated May Ist, 1918, have hereunto set their hands and seals on this 21st day of June in the year one thousand nine hundred and eighteen; and the State Street Trust Company has caused its corporate name to be hereto subscribed by Ashton L. Carr, its Vice-President, and its corporate seal to be affixed by Dana M. Dutch, its Secretary, thereunto duly authorized. Albert J. Lovett, Roger Ernst, Trustees of the 123 Portland Street Trust under an Agreement and Declaration of Trust dated May 1, 1918, but not individually and each a seal. State Street Trust Company by Ashton L. Carr, Vice-President, and Dana M. Dutch, Secretary, and the Corporate Seal. Signed, sealed, and in presence of Henry Palmer, CommonWEALTH OF Massachusetts. Suffolk ss. On this 21st day of June, 1918, personally appeared Albert J. Lovett and Roger Ernst, Trustees of the 123 Portland Street Trust under an Agreement and Declaration of Trust dated May Ist, 1918, and acknowledged the foregoing instrument to be their free act and deed as such Trustees before me, Henry W. Palmer. Notary Public. Notarial Seal. CommoNWEALTH OF Massachusetts, Suffolk, ss. On this 21st day of June, 1918 personally appeared Ashton L. Carr,

<br>

### --- Page 0581 ---

APPENDIX OF FORMS

Vice-President and Dana M. Duren, Secretary of the State Street Trust Company and acknowledged the foregoing instrument to be the free act and deed of said State Street Trust Company, before me—Henry W. Palmer, Notary Public. My commission expires August, 1919, and his Notarial Seal.

PORTLAND STREET TRUST

LEASE

This INDENTURE made this first day of May, in the year one thousand nine hundred eighteen, by and between ALBERT J. Loverr of Norwell, Plymouth County, and Rocrr Ernst, of Cambridge, Middlesex County, both in the Commonwealth of Massachusetts, Trustees under an Agreement and Declaration of Trust dated the first day of May, 1918, recorded in Suffolk Registry of Deeds, Book... Page... known as the “123 Portland Street Trust” hereinafter called the “Lessors” which expression shall include their successors and assigns, where the context so admits, of the one part, and the Portland Street Garage, Inc., a corporation duly incorporated under the laws of the said Commonwealth of Massachusetts, hereinafter called the “Lessee” which expression shall include its successors and assigns, where the context so admits, of the other part. Witnesseth: That in consideration of the rent and covenants herein reserved and contained on the part of the Lessee to be paid, performed and observed, the Lessors do hereby demise and lease unto the Lessee all that parcel of land with the building to be erected thereon, situate on Portland Street running through to Friend Street, and being numbered 119 to 127 on Portland Street and 190 to 200 on Friend Street, as shown on a plan by William A. Johnson, surveyor, dated December 30, 1916, and recorded in Suffolk Registry of Deeds, all in the City of Boston, containing in all about 11,848 square feet, with all the rights, easements, and appurtenances to the same belonging, and usually had and enjoyed therewith, and all upon and subject to the terms, conditions and provisions herein contained. The Lessors shall forthwith, after the delivery of this lease duly executed by the parties hereto, begin and with all reasonable expedition thereafter proceed to erect upon the demised premises a new building at least six stories in height, in accordance with the plans and specifications for said new building prepared by Andrews, Rantoul & Jones, architects, which shall be submitted to and approved by the Lessees so far as the general design, structure and plans are concerned including fixtures, furnishings: said new building shall conform to the building laws of the City of Boston and to all laws, ordinances, rules and regulations of all public authorities

<br>

### --- Page 0582 ---

123 PORTLAND STREET TRUST

and of the Boston Board of Fire Underwriters. To Have and To Hold the said demised premises unto the Lessee for the term of twenty (20) years, beginning with noon of the first day after the building is completed and ready for occupancy, as declared by the architect, with the sanction of the builder. Yielding and Paying therefor the rent and sums due as herein stipulated, in gold coin of the United States of the present standard of weight and fineness, or, at the option of the Lessors, in currency, during the said term of twenty (20) years, by equal monthly payments, on the first day of each month for the month ending at noon on that day, with the exception of the first payment as hereinafter provided, and at the same rate for such further time as the said Lessee or any other person or persons claiming under the Lessee shall hold the said premises or any part thereof. The Lessee shall not, however, be required to pay any rent for a period ending thirty (30) days after the date of the completion of the building, and the first payment shall be made on the first day of the first calendar month sixty (60) days or more after said date of completion of the building as above provided, and shall cover the rent due from the date thirty (30) days after the completion of the building to the date of such payment. The rent to be paid during said term shall be computed as follows: Four and one-half (4 1/2) per cent. on the value of the land, which shall be taken at two hundred thousand (200,000) dollars, plus seven (7) per cent. of the actual cost of the construction of the building, including architects' fees, builders' profits, and actual carrying charges, including interest and prorated taxes during the time of construction. Provided, however, that the rent, exclusive of the amounts hereinafter provided to be paid as additional rent, in no case shall be less than twenty-three thousand (23,000) dollars per annum, nor more than twenty-seven thousand (27,000) dollars per annum. The limitations stated in the foregoing proviso shall not, however, apply to the rental to be paid under any renewal of this lease. The Lessee shall have an option for a renewal of said lease for a further term of ten (10) years, such option to be exercised at least three (3) months before the termination of this lease, by mailing postpaid, or delivering to the Lessors a written notice of the desire of the Lessee to renew said lease. If not so exercised, it shall be forfeited. In case of such extension the same percentages upon the value of the land and the cost of the building shall be used as a factor for fixing the rent for such further term, but a revaluation of the land and building to be used as bases for the computation of the rent shall be made. If the Lessors and the Lessee shall be unable to agree upon such valuation, it shall be made by three disinterested appraisers, chosen, one by the Lessors, one by the Lessee, and the

<br>

### --- Page 0583 ---

APPENDIX OF FORMS

third by the two so chosen. These appraisers shall fix the fair market value of the land and of the buildings as of the date of the end of the term of this lease. The decision of a majority of the appraisers shall be final. In case of the refusal or neglect of either party to appoint an appraiser within twenty (20) days after the date of the receipt by the Lessors of notice of Lessee’s desire to renew, the appraiser chosen by the other party may determine said valuations, and his decision shall be final and binding upon both parties. In case of the failure of the two appraisers to agree upon a third within thirty (30) days after the date of the receipt of said notice, application may be made by either party to one of the Justices of the Superior Court of the Commonwealth of Massachusetts, to make such appointment. The Lessee shall deposit with the Lessors, from time to time in proportion as the building progresses as may be agreed upon, during the period of the construction of the building, the sum of twenty thousand (20,000) dollars. Said sum is to be used toward defraying the cost of construction of the building. The Lessee is to be allotted therefor either shares of the stock or bonds issued by said 123 Portland Street Trust of an aggregate face value of twenty thousand (20,000) dollars, in proportions to be hereafter agreed upon, which stock or bonds, or both, as the case may be, shall be held by the Lessors as collateral security for the full performance by the Lessee of the terms of the lease, the Lessee to execute such powers of attorney, assignments, or other instruments as may be required to put such stock and/or bonds in negotiable form. At the beginning of the last year of the term of the original lease or of any extension thereof, said sum of twenty thousand (20,000) dollars shall be credited on account of the rent for that year, the balance of the rent for that year to be paid in equal monthly instalments during the last three months of the term. In case the Lessee shall exercise its option to renew said lease at some time subsequent to the beginning of the last year of the term of the original lease, it shall forthwith pay to the Lessors in cash a sum equal to the amount of rent on account of which any of said twenty thousand (20,000) dollars may have been credited, and thereafter no further such credit shall be made on account of rent until the beginning of the last year of the term of the renewal of said lease. As soon as said sum of twenty thousand (20,000) dollars shall have been so credited, the title to the stock and/or bonds held by the Lessors as collateral security shall pass to the Lessors, and the Lessee shall forthwith execute such assignments, releases, discharges, or other instruments as the Lessors may reasonably request to fully vest in them the title to said securities. Until the title to such stock and/or bonds shall

<br>

### --- Page 0584 ---

123 PORTLAND STREET TRUST

so pass to the Lessors, the Lessee, unless in default under any of the terms of this lease, shall be entitled to receive all interest on any such bonds and all dividends on any such stock. In case of the happening of any event or the existence of any condition whereby as hereinafter provided, the Lessors would have a right of entry hereunder, the Lessors may, at one time or from time to time sell at Broker’s Board, or at public auction or at private sale, the said securities, as a whole or in such lots as they may determine, without notice; and the Lessors may become the purchasers at any such sale, if at public auction or at Broker’s Board. After deducting all reasonable legal or other costs and expenses of sale and all reasonable expenses incurred in protecting the collateral, and all amounts due or to become due from the Lessee under any of the provisions of this lease, together with interest at the rate of six (6) per cent. per annum on any overdue payments, any residue of the proceeds of such sale or sales shall be paid to the Lessee or to whosoever may be lawfully entitled to receive the same. The said Lessee hereby covenants and promises with and to the said Lessors that the Lessee will, during the said term and for such further time as the Lessee or those claiming under the Lessee shall hold the said premises, or any part thereof, pay unto the Lessors the said rent at the times and in the manner aforesaid, and also in each year during the term of this lease will pay unto the Lessors at least ten (10) days before they are due and payable all taxes levied by any governmental authority, whether in the nature of taxes now in being or not, which may be payable for or in respect of the said land and building or any part thereof, or for or in respect of the rent or income thereof, including Federal and State Income Taxes, whether assessed upon the Lessors or their beneficiaries or upon the Lessee, but not including any taxes assessed upon any of the said parties for or in respect of any other property or income, (taxes for the first and last years of the term hereof to be apportioned on the basis of the assessment for that year), and all charges for gas, water, electricity or other agencies, used on the demised premises as the same become due, and all assessments thereon except assessments for betterments; the cost of any structural additions that may be ordered by any governmental authorities having jurisdiction shall be paid by the Lessors, but in such case and in case of any assessments for betterments the Lessee shall also, from the date when such additions or betterments are actually paid for by the Lessors and during the remainder of the term of this lease or of any extension thereof, pay, in equal instalments with the rent hereinbefore reserved, additional rent at the rate of seven (7) per cent. per annum, upon all payments made by the

<br>

### --- Page 0585 ---

APPENDIX OF FORMS

Lessors for such additions or betterments; it being the intention of the parties hereto and it being hereby agreed that said rent hereinbefore reserved shall be absolutely net to the Lessors, free from deductions of any name or nature; and the Lessee will keep all and singular the said premises, and the glass and fixtures in and upon the same, from and after the erection of said new building in such good repair, order and condition, externally and internally, as the same then are or may be put in during the term, reasonable use and damage by accidental fire or other unavoidable casualty only excepted, and will keep the same thoroughly inspected, provided with all safety guards and appliances, and in such condition as to comply from time to time with all laws, ordinances, rules, and regulations of all public authorities and of the Boston Board of Fire Underwriters, whether applicable to the demised premises as such or to the Lessors or Lessee with respect thereto, except that the cost of any structural additions necessary to comply with such rules and regulations shall be paid by the Lessors as hereinbefore provided; it being understood that the taking possession of said premises by the Lessee shall be conclusive as against the Lessee that the said premises were in good and satisfactory condition when possession was so taken; and will save harmless and indemnify the Lessors from all loss, damage, liability and expense incurred, suffered or claimed by reason of any neglect or use of the demised premises or of anything thereon, or any neglect, use or escape of gas, water, steam, electricity, or other agency, or by reason of snow or ice, or by reason of any injury, loss or damage to any person or property upon the demised premises or the streets, alleyway, or sidewalks adjacent thereto; will be answerable for all nuisance caused or suffered on said premises, and will remove snow and ice from said premises, its sidewalks and approaches bounding thereon. It is expressly understood and agreed that during said term said Lessee is to be to all intents and purposes as to all liabilities and responsibilities in the place of the owners of said premises, except as to the payment of betterments and of structural additions of the kind above mentioned. After the completion of said new building, as hereinbefore set forth, no structural alterations or additions shall be made during the term aforesaid, in or to the same, without the consent of the Lessors being first obtained in writing, allowing thereof, except as herein agreed; if any such alterations or additions are made they shall become a part of the premises and the property of the Lessors. The Lessee shall not suffer or permit any strip or waste or overloading damaging or defacing of the said premises, or any use thereof which shall be unlawful, improper, offensive, or contrary to any law of the Commonwealth or ordinance or by

<br>

### --- Page 0586 ---

law of the city of Boston, or rule or regulation of any public authority for the time being in force, or injurious to any person or property; the Lessee covenants that no act or thing shall be done upon the said premises which may make void or voidable any insurance of the said premises or any part thereof; and the Lessee further covenants that it will not assign this lease, nor sublet the whole or any part (other than the stores on the ground floor) more than two floors of said premises, without first obtaining on each occasion the written consent of the Lessors; should said Lessee assign this lease or sublet the whole or any part (other than said stores) more than two floors of said premises without such written consent, neither acceptance of rent by the Lessors from the Lessee or other person thereafter, nor failure on the part of the Lessors for any particular period to take action on account of such breach, or to enforce their rights, shall be deemed a waiver of the breach, but the same shall be a continuing breach as long as such subtenancy or occupancy continues. The Lessors and their agents or representatives may, during the Lessee’s occupation of the said premises, at seasonable times enter into and upon the same to examine the condition thereof or to make repairs if they shall desire to do so. The Lessee will, upon the expiration or other termination of this lease, remove all goods and effects from the demised premises not the property of the Lessors, and will yield up to the Lessors all and singular the said demised premises and all erections and additions to and upon the same in good and tenantable repair, order, and condition in all respects, reasonable use thereof and damage by fire or other unavoidable casualty only excepted. The Lessee further covenants that after the completion of said new building it will pay to the Lessors the premiums of insurance as they become due for keeping said premises insured against fire to the amount of at least eighty (80) per cent. of their full insurable value as the same may be fixed from time to time by an appraiser or appraisers approved by the Boston Board of Fire Underwriters or other board or authority exercising powers similar to those now exercised by the Boston Board of Fire Underwriters, and for insurance against loss of rent in case of fire or other unavoidable casualty to the amount of at least eighty (80) per cent. of the full rental value of said premises, such insurance to be placed by and to be kept in possession of the Lessors or their agents, appointees or assignees, and to be payable to them or to such other party or parties as they may designate. The Lessee further agrees to pay to the Lessors the premiums as they become due on any insurance that may be effected by the Lessors insuring the Lessors and/

<br>

### --- Page 0587 ---

or the Lessee, as their interest may appear, for damages on account of personal injury, including death resulting therefrom accidentally suffered by any person or persons whomsoever while within or upon the demised premises or ways adjacent thereto. And the Lessee further agrees to pay to the Lessors the premiums as they become due on boiler insurance, so called, that may be effected by the Lessors, to a reasonable amount, insuring the Lessors and/or the Lessee and/or others, as their interest may appear, against loss on account of damage or injury resulting from boiler explosion. All such policies shall be kept in possession of the Lessors or their agents, appointees, or assignees, and all premiums thereon shall be paid by the Lessee. Provided also that in case said premises or any part thereof, exceeding forty (40) per cent. of the full insurable value thereof as fixed for the purposes of insurance as above provided, shall be destroyed by fire or other unavoidable casualty or shall be taken by right of eminent domain or by other authority of law after the execution hereof and before the expiration of the said term, then this lease, and the said term shall terminate at the election of the Lessors; and if they shall not so elect, then in case of any such taking or destruction of or damage to the demised premises, a just proportion of the rent hereinbefore reserved, according to the nature and extent of the injury sustained by the demised premises, shall be abated until the demised premises, or, in case of such taking, whatever may remain thereof, shall have been put in proper condition for use and occupation. The Lessee agrees promptly to notify the Lessors or their agents for the receipt of rent in case of any fire or accident upon the leased premises. In case of any such taking, the total damages arising therefrom shall be recovered by the Lessors, the Lessee hereby assigning all rights in or to such damages to the Lessors, and the net amount recoverable and recovered therefor shall be apportioned between the Lessors and the Lessee in such proportions as shall be determined by them, or if they should be unable to agree, then as shall be determined by arbitrators to represent their respective interest, taking into account the value of the land at the time of taking, the value of the new building erected by the Lessors, the portion of the term of the lease then unexpired, and any other relevant facts. The Lessee shall receive his proportion of the net amount recovered determined as aforesaid. The arbitrators to apportion the damages as between the Lessors and the Lessee shall in every case be chosen as follows, namely: one by the Lessors, one by the Lessee and a third by the two so chosen. The determination by a majority of the three arbitrators so chosen shall be final and conclusive upon the parties hereto, and the entire cost and expense of such arbitration, including the services of the

<br>

### --- Page 0588 ---

123 PORTLAND STREET TRUST

arbitrators, shall be paid out of the amount recovered with respect to such taking and shall be a prior charge on the amount so recovered, and only the balance of such amounts shall be the subject of the apportionment above provided. It is the intention of the parties by the preceding clause relating to the distribution of damages or indemnity for taking by public authority, to appoint the Lessors as the persons to whom such damages or indemnity are to be paid as provided in Chapter 344, Part 2, Section 18, of the General Acts of 1917, and to further provide that such sums, instead of being held in trust, as provided in said Section 18, shall be immediately distributed in the manner herein provided for. This lease is made on condition also that, if the Lessee shall neglect or fail to perform or observe any of the covenants herein contained on the Lessee's part to be performed or observed, or if the estate hereby created shall be taken on execution or other process of law, or if any assignment shall be made of the Lessee's property for the benefit of creditors, or if a receiver or other similar officer shall be appointed to take charge of any property of or to wind up the affairs of the Lessee, or if the Lessee shall be declared bankrupt or insolvent according to law, then, and in any of the said cases the Lessor lawfully may, immediately or at any time thereafter and without demand or notice, enter into and upon the demised premises, or any part thereof in the name of the whole, and repossess the same of the Lessors' former estate, and expel the Lessee and those claiming through or under the Lessee, and remove the effects of both or either (forcibly if necessary) without being deemed guilty of any manner of trespass and without prejudice to any remedies for arrears of rent or preceding breach of covenant, and upon entry as aforesaid, the Lessee's estate shall end; and, notwithstanding any other provisions of this lease, the Lessors shall forthwith upon such termination be entitled to recover damages for such breach in an amount equal to the amount of the rent reserved in this lease for the residue of the term hereof, less the fair rental value of the premises for the residue of said term. The Lessors shall be similarly entitled to recover damages in case of termination of any renewal of this lease. In case of the eviction of said Lessee, or in case of the termination of this lease, or any extension thereof for any cause whatsoever, the Lessors may immediately recover of the Lessee the pro rata rent up to the time of eviction or termination, irrespective of the periods herein prescribed for the payment of rent.
