The Lessee further covenants and agrees with the Lessors that in case said Lessee shall vacate said premises during the term of this lease, the Lessors may, at their option, without terminating this lease, enter into said premises and re-let the same for the account of the Lessee for such rent and upon such terms as

<br>

### --- Page 0589 ---

the Lessors shall approve; and if a sufficient sum shall not be thus realized monthly (after paying the expenses of such re-letting and of collecting the rent accruing from such re-letting) to satisfy the monthly rent above provided to be paid by the Lessee, then the Lessee will satisfy and pay such deficiency upon demand monthly. Any assent, expressed or implied, by the Lessors to any breach of any covenant or condition herein contained, or any waiver, express or implied, by the Lessors of any such covenant or condition herein contained, shall operate as such only in the specific instance and shall not be construed as an assent or waiver of any such covenant or condition generally or of any subsequent breach thereof, and any notice from the Lessors relating to the demised premises or the occupancy thereof or any provision of or matter arising under this Indenture, shall be deemed duly served if left at the demised premises addressed to the Lessee, or if mailed, postage prepaid, addressed to the Lessee at its last address known to the Lessors. The Lessee further covenants to pay all reasonable attorneys' fees and expenses of the Lessors incurred in enforcing any of the obligations of the Lessee under this lease, or in any litigation or negotiation in which the Lessors shall, without their fault, become involved through or on account of this lease. It is mutually agreed between said Lessors and said Lessee that each of the provisions of this lease shall extend to and shall, as the case may require, bind and inure to the benefit of not only the Lessors and the Lessee, but also their heirs, legal representatives, successors and assigns. It is further mutually agreed that if the Lessors are described herein as trustees or in any other representative capacity, neither the Lessors nor any one of them nor the shareholders or beneficiaries under the agreement or declaration of trust, or other instrument under which the Lessors hold title to the leased premises, nor any of them, shall in any way be held personally liable hereunder, and that claims against the Lessors by reason of this lease or the ownership or management of the leased premises shall be made only against the said premises.

In Witness Whereof the said Albert J. Lovett and Roger Ernst, Trustees as aforesaid, set their hands and seals, and the Portland Street Garage, Inc. has caused these presents to be executed in its name and behalf by its duly authorized officer and its seal to be attached hereto and to a duplicate hereof, on the day and year first above written. Albert J. Lovett, Roger Ernst, as Trustees but not individually and each a seal. Portland Street Garage, Inc. By Asa H. Weeks, President, Lemur. C. Woopsury, Treasurer, and the Corporate Seal. Commonwealth of Massachusetts, Suffolk, ss, on this twenty-first day of June, 1918, personally appeared Albert J. Lovett and Roger Ernst, Trustees of the

<br>

### --- Page 0590 ---

123 PORTLAND STREET TRUST

123 Portland Street Trust under an agreement and declaration of trust dated May 1, 1918, and acknowledged the foregoing instrument to be their free act and deed as such trustees. Before me William Hall Best, Notary Public. My Commission expires Jan. 24, 1924. And his notarial seal. Boston, Mass., Feb. 27, 1918.

Special Meeting of the Board of Directors of the Portland Street Garage, Inc. Held at room forty-six (46) Clinton Street, Boston, Mass. on Feb. 27, 1918, at 12:30 P. M. Present: Lemuel C. Woodbury, Asa H. Weeks, Thorwold C. Lillemoen, being all of the Directors of the Portland Street Garage, Inc. The meeting was called to order by its president, Asa H. Weeks. Vote of the Portland Street Garage, Inc. A discussion was entered into as to the advisability of leasing real estate on Portland Street in Boston. It was unanimously voted: That Asa H. Weeks, President, and Lemuel C. Woodbury, Clerk, be and are hereby authorized and empowered to act for the Board of Directors of the Portland Street Garage, Inc., and to sign and execute all papers and documents necessary to the leasing of the land and garage building, when completed, at No. 190 to 200 on Friend Street, and No. 119 to 127 on Portland Street. Voted to adjourn. Lemuel C. Woodbury, Clerk.

A true copy: ATTEST: Lemuel C. Woodbury and the Corporate Seal. Boston, Mass., June 20, 1918. Then personally appeared the above named Lemuel C. Woodbury, Clerk of the Portland Street Garage, Inc., and made oath that the above vote is a true copy of the vote taken on February 27, 1918. Before me, Alvah G. Sleeper, Justice of the Peace. June 21, 1918.

<br>

### --- Page 0591 ---

LUDLOW MANUFACTURING ASSOCIATES

SUPPLEMENTARY DECLARATION OF TRUST

Whereas the undersigned, Trustees under a Declaration of Trust of 1 January, 1902, deposited with the Old Colony Trust Company of Boston and amended by two Supplementary Declarations respectively dated 14 January, 1904, and 19 December, 1911, also so deposited, hereby propose to further amend said Declaration as already amended as above stated, by a Supplementary Declaration of Trust in which shall be embodied all provisions of the Trust as existing at the date of this instrument;

THE UNDERSIGNED TRUSTEES HEREBY DECLARE that all the property and shares now held as aforesaid by them as such Trustees, together with any hereafter acquired by them under the provisions of this instrument, is and is to be held for the account and benefit of the holders from time to time of the certificates of shares issued hereunder, and is and is to be held, used, managed, and administered upon the trusts and in the manner following:—

ARTICLE I

The said Trustees, designating themselves so far as may be convenient by the name of the “Ludlow Manufacturing Associates,” may use and employ the trust property and assets:

First. In the manufacture at Ludlow, Springfield, or Wilbraham, of flax, hemp, jute, cotton, wool, silk, and other fibres, and in the manufacture of paper and carpets, and of machinery or other articles composed in whole or in part of wood or metal—the said manufacturing enterprises to be carried on either directly or through corporations or other organizations in which the Trustees own a controlling interest;

Second. In investments in the shares of any electric light, railroad, or power company operating in whole or in part in the city of Springfield, or in the towns of Palmer, Chicopee, Ludlow, and Wilbraham, and in contracts with any such company or any other company for the purchase or sale of electricity;

Third. In establishing at any other place or places within or without the Commonwealth industries similar to those now or hereafter established on their properties in Ludlow, Springfield, or Wilbraham whenever the interests of said last named industries may render such course advisable—such establishment to be effected by new construction, by the purchase in whole

<br>

### --- Page 0592 ---

LUDLOW MANUFACTURING ASSOCIATES

or in part of existing plants, or by giving in exchange for the stock or securities of any corporation or other organization other securities owned by the Trustees or shares issued under this instrument;

Fourth. In the purchase, sale, renting, or leasing of real estate and power of any description, as the interests of the Trust may from time to time require, and in improving and developing any real estate or water power held by them by the building of storage reservoirs, the erection of buildings, the construction of streets and sewers, and by all other methods conducive in the judgment of the Trustees, to the wise and profitable use of said real estate or of said water power.

To enable the Trustees to fully execute this Trust, they are hereby empowered :—

1. To carry on any business above described according to their discretion and to employ therein such agents or agencies as they may deem expedient;

2. To pay all taxes, assessments, and necessary expenses;

3. To buy any property, real or personal, including shares or obligations issued hereunder, and any rights, franchises, privileges, or securities which the conduct of any business above described may in their judgment require, or which may in their judgment tend to promote its successful prosecution or the interest of the shareholders, and to hold, use, lease, or sell the trust property or any part thereof (except as hereinafter provided) at their discretion;

4. To borrow money for any business above described or for the purchase of any property herein authorized and to give notes, make contracts of guaranty or suretyship, or enter into other obligations therefor, and to pledge the personal property of the Trust or any part thereof (except as hereinafter provided) to secure such notes or obligations or any contract entered into in the course of the execution of this Trust; provided, however, that all notes or obligations given for money borrowed shall bear the written approval of at least one Trustee in addition to the signature of the Treasurer or other authorized officer;

5. To create a reserve fund by investing in any securities that are legal investments for the savings banks of the Commonwealth of Massachusetts or of the State of New York; also to make loans upon such collateral securities as are prescribed by statute in the case of loans by the savings banks of the Commonwealth of Massachusetts or of the State of New York;

6. To exercise exclusive control and management of the trust property; to vote in person or by proxy upon all shares of stock belonging to the Trust and to collect and receipt for any dividends

<br>

### --- Page 0593 ---

APPENDIX OF FORMS

Dividends thereon, provided, however, that shares issued hereunder and purchased by the Trustees for the account of the Trust shall not, so long as they belong to the Trust, either receive dividends or be voted at any meeting of shareholders; to contract with any company controlled by them, to begin and defend legal proceedings, employ counsel, and compromise or arbitrate claims and generally to do all acts and things necessary and proper for the complete execution of this Trust and the protection of the interests of shareholders therein, provided, however, that the Trustees shall have no power to bind the shareholders personally by any contract, express or implied, or by any act, neglect, or default; that neither Trustees nor shareholders shall be personally liable on any such contract or for any such act, neglect, or default, and that any party to such contract or injured by such act, neglect, or default shall have recourse for satisfaction, payment, or indemnity solely to the trust estate; that for any judgment recovered against and paid by the Trustees they shall be entitled to reimburse themselves from the trust estate; and that every note, bond, obligation, or contract in writing made or given by the Trustees shall, by explicit reference to this Supplementary Declaration of Trust, give notice of the limitations upon the power of the Trustees and of the exemption from personal liability of both Trustees and shareholders and shall contain an express declaration that no recourse shall be had in any event to any Trustee or shareholder.

No sale or mortgage of the real estate of the Trust nor of any of its industrial establishments nor of the stock of the Ludlow Manufacturing Company, and no lease of real estate or power belonging to the Trust and exceeding the annual rental value of Ten thousand dollars ($10,000), shall be made by the Trustees until the same shall have been first approved by the vote of two-thirds in interest of the shares entitled to vote under this Supplementary Declaration of Trust and voted by the holders thereof in person or by proxy at an annual or special meeting notified as herein prescribed and the call for which shall state that such sale, mortgage, or lease, is to be acted upon at said meeting.

Shares issued hereunder and bought by the Trustees for the account of the Trust shall be reported to the shareholders at the next annual meeting after such purchase and be disposed of as said meeting may determine.

ARTICLE II

A majority of the Trustees shall constitute a quorum, and any action taken at a meeting at which a quorum is present and which meeting has been duly notified in the manner previously

<br>

### --- Page 0594 ---

LUDLOW MANUFACTURING ASSOCIATES

prescribed by the Trustees shall be operative and effective as the act of all the Trustees; provided, however, that the approval of six (6) Trustees given at a duly notified meeting shall be necessary for action on all matters relating to the loaning of money, the absorption of new plants either by purchase or otherwise, the starting of new industries in Ludlow, Springfield, or Wilbraham, and the establishment of branch industries elsewhere.

In no event shall any purchaser be bound to see to the application of the purchase money or other consideration received or realized upon the execution of any deed, bill of sale, mortgage, transfer, or other conveyance authorized as aforesaid, and so far as strangers to the Trust are concerned, a resolution of the Trustees certified as such by their Secretary and authorizing a particular act to be done shall be conclusive evidence that such act is within the powers of the Trustees.

The Trustees shall annually elect from among their number a President, and shall elect from among their number or otherwise a Treasurer, Secretary, and, in their discretion, Vice-Presidents, Assistant Treasurers, Assistant Secretaries, and such other officers or agents as they may deem advisable, and may act in any manner by or through any such officer or agent.

The Trustees may adopt and from time to time amend or repeal by-laws for the conduct of their business, and in such by-laws or in regulations adopted at any meeting may define duties of their officers, agents, servants and representatives; provided, however, that a by-law shall be repealed or amended only at a meeting the notice of which specifies that the matter of such repeal or amendment is to be acted upon.

- ARTICLE III

The title to the trust property of every description and the right to the conduct of any business hereinbefore described are vested exclusively in the Trustees, so that shareholders are without interest therein other than that conferred by their shares issued hereunder, and shall have no right to call for any partition, or division of property, profits, rights, or interests.

Shares shall be personal property giving only the rights in this instrument and in the certificates thereof specifically set forth. The death of a shareholder during the continuance of this Trust shall not terminate the Trust nor give his or her legal representatives a right to an account or to take any action in the courts or otherwise against other shareholders or the Trustees, but shall simply entitle the legal representatives of the deceased to demand and receive a new certificate of shares in place of the certificate held by the deceased, upon the acceptance of which such

<br>

### --- Page 0595 ---

APPENDIX OF FORMS

legal representatives shall succeed to all the rights of the deceased under this Trust.

ARTICLE IV
the shareholders such proportion of the net profits arising from the conduct of the affairs of the Trust as they deem proper; such division may be made among the shareholders of record at the time of declaring a dividend, or among the shareholders as they may deem desirable to use in the conduct of its affairs or to retain for future requirements or extensions of

ARTICLE V

The shares issuable hereunder by the Trustees shall be one hundred thousand (100,000) in all. Said shares shall be evidenced by a negotiable certificate or certificates in the form following :—

(Form of CERTIFICATE OF SHARES)
LUDLOW MANUFACTURING ASSOCIATES

No. Shares.

This certifies that...., the holder of....(..) shares of the property held and administered in trust by Trustees acting as convenient under the name of the Ludlow Manufacturing Associates and in accordance with and subject to a Supplementary Declaration of Trust dated 20th January, 1914, which is on file with the Old Colony Trust Company and which is hereby referred to and made a part of this certificate.

This certificate must be signed by the Old Colony Trust Company, Transfer Agent, and no transfer hereof shall be effectual as regards the Ludlow Manufacturing Associates until this certificate.

<br>

### --- Page 0596 ---

LUDLOW MANUFACTURING ASSOCIATES

certificate has been surrendered and the transfer recorded upon the books of said Transfer Agent.

In Witness WHEREOF the Trustees under said Supplementary Declaration of Trust herein designated as the Ludlow Manufacturing Associates have caused their common seal to be hereto affixed and this certificate to be executed in their name and behalf by their President and attested by their Secretary this . day of...., 19...

LUDLOW MANUFACTURING ASSOCIATES

President.
Attest: Secretary.
Signed

OLD COLONY TRUST COMPANY, Transfer Agent,
By... Assistant Secretary.
By... Transfer Clerk.
(Form of TRANSFER OF SHARES)

For VALUE RECEIVED...... hereby sell, assign, transfer, and deliver to........ the within named shares of the Ludlow Manufacturing Associates and request that said transfer be recorded on the books of said Transfer Agent.
Witness... hand..this....day of....19...

The Trustees shall keep books of record of the certificates of shares originally issued hereunder and of all transfers thereof. Upon any transfer thereof, a new certificate or new certificates shall be issued, being first recorded and signed by the Transfer Agent appointed by the Trustees, and only shareholders whose certificates are so recorded shall be entitled to vote, or to collect dividends, or to otherwise exercise and enjoy the rights of shareholders.

Each shareholder shall in writing notify the Treasurer of the Trustees of his post-office address—which may be changed by a like notice—and in the absence of any such notice from a shareholder his post-office address shall be taken to be Boston. The Treasurer shall file a memorandum of the addresses of shareholders with the Transfer Agent and keep it informed of any changes therein.

In case of the loss, mutilation, or destruction of a certificate, the Trustees may issue a new one upon such terms as they see fit.

<br>

### --- Page 0597 ---

APPENDIX OF FORMS

ARTICLE VI

The Trustees may, from time to time, with the consent of the holders of two-thirds in interest of the shares entitled to vote under this Supplementary Declaration of Trust and voted at a meeting the call for which shall contain specific notice of the proposition to be submitted, issue and dispose of either additional common shares or preferred shares for such purpose, on such terms and in such manner as the shareholders at such meeting may decide. They may, with the like consent voted at a meeting called in the like manner convey the trust property to other Trustees or to a corporation already existing or to be organized by the Trustees.

ARTICLE VII

The Trustees shall always be eleven (11) in number and shall be divided into three (3) classes, two of which shall consist of four (4) Trustees and one of which shall consist of three (3) Trustees. The term of each respective class shall expire at the time fixed herein for the annual meeting of the shareholders to be held in the third year after the election of each respective class, provided that the class the term of which expires at the time fixed for the annual meeting to be held in 1922 shall consist of three (3) members, that the class the term of which expires at the time fixed for the annual meeting to be held in 1923 shall consist of four (4) members, and that the class the term of which expires at the time fixed for the annual meeting to be held in 1924 shall consist of four (4) members, and provided further that the present Trustees as well as any Trustees hereafter elected shall in all cases hold office until their successors have been elected and have accepted the Trust. A Trustee may resign by written notice delivered or mailed to the Secretary or President and such resignation shall take effect immediately or at a later date according to the terms of said notice. Where by amendment to the Declaration of Trust the number of Trustees in any class or classes is increased, the additional Trustee or Trustees necessary to fill any such class or classes shall be elected by the shareholders at the meeting at which such amendment is adopted for a term expiring with the date of expiration of the class to which each respective additional Trustee is so elected.

Stated meetings of the Trustees shall be held at least once a month, and other meetings shall be held from time to time upon the call of the President or any three (3) of the Trustees. The vote of an absent Trustee may be counted provided that the vote in the form entered upon the records is signed by him and attached to the records, and for the purpose of passing said vote

<br>

### --- Page 0598 ---

but no other, he may be considered as present if necessary to form a quorum.

The Trustees shall not be liable for errors of judgment either in holding property originally conveyed to them or in acquiring and afterwards holding additional property, or for any loss resulting from any investment, or from any act or omission to act performed or omitted by them in the execution of this Trust in good faith. They shall not be liable for the acts or omissions of any officer, agent, or servant appointed by or acting for them, nor be obliged to give any bond to secure the due discharge of their Trust, nor shall any Trustee be liable for any act or default of any other Trustee.

ARTICLE VIII

The fiscal year of the Trustees shall end on the last Saturday of December or the first Saturday of January, whichever may be nearest the thirty-first of December. Each annual meeting shall be held in Boston on the third Tuesday in February of each year, beginning with the year 1918, of which meeting notice shall be given by the Secretary by mail to each shareholder at his registered address at least ten days before said meeting.

At each annual meeting of the shareholders, the Trustees shall make a full report upon the affairs of the Trust, and upon its business and operations during the year preceding, together with the statement of its financial standing as shown by the books and accounts of the Treasurer.

At each annual meeting shareholders shall elect the requisite number of Trustees, in accordance with Article VII hereof, to serve for a term expiring at the time fixed herein for the annual meeting to be held in the third year after such election.

Upon the election of any Trustee by the shareholders or by the remaining Trustees, he shall execute an acceptance of this trust, which, together with a certificate of the Secretary of the Trustees of the election of such Trustee, shall be forthwith filed with the Old Colony Trust Company or other depositary at that time having the custody of this instrument.

In case of the death, resignation, or inability to act of any Trustee, the remaining Trustees shall forthwith fill the vacancy for the unexpired term. Upon any election of Trustees by the shareholders, or by the remaining Trustees in the case of vacancy, the trust estate upon the acceptance of the Trust by the new Trustees or Trustee shall vest in them or him and the continuing Trustees without any further act or conveyance.

While any vacancy exists in the office of Trustee from what

<br>

### --- Page 0599 ---

APPENDIX OF FORMS

ever cause, the continuing or surviving Trustees or Trustee shall have all the powers and discharge all the duties granted or imposed by this instrument.

Each Trustee hereunder shall be the holder of at least Ten (10) shares and shall have the right to purchase at any public or private sale any shares or securities issued hereunder.

Special meetings of shareholders may be called at any time upon ten (10) days’ notice given as above stated when ordered by the President or the Trustees. At all meetings of the shareholders each holder of shares, except as provided in Article I, Section Fourth, Paragraph 6, shall be entitled to one (1) vote for each share held by him, and any shareholder may vote by proxy.

No business shall be transacted at any special meeting of shareholders unless notice of such business has been given in the call for the meeting.

No business except to adjourn shall be transacted at any meeting of shareholders unless a majority in interest of the shares entitled to vote under this Supplementary Declaration of Trust are represented by the holders thereof in person or by proxy.

ARTICLE IX

This Trust, unless sooner terminated by the shareholders in the manner hereinafter provided, shall continue until the first day of January, 1950, unless all of said above named Trustees and all successors to them now living shall have died more than twenty-one (21) years before said date, in which event this Trust shall terminate at the expiration of twenty-one (21) years from the death of the last survivor of said Trustees and said successors. At the termination of the Trust the Trustees shall wind up the affairs and business of the Trust and, after paying and satisfying all obligations and liabilities thereof, shall divide the property then in their hands or its net proceeds ratably among the shareholders.

ARTICLE X

This Supplementary Declaration of Trust may be altered or amended or this Trust terminated by the vote of two-thirds in interest of the shares entitled to vote hereunder and voted by the holders thereof in person or by proxy at any meeting of the shareholders duly notified pursuant to Article VIII by a call for such meeting in which it is specifically stated that such termination or such alteration or amendment is to be acted upon. In case of a vote in favor of such termination or of such alteration or amendment as the case may be, the President and the Secretary of such meeting shall certify such vote and any alterations

<br>

### --- Page 0600 ---

LUDLOW MANUFACTURING ASSOCIATES

or amendments so adopted in writing to the said Trustees, and, when the Transfer Agent has certified to the Trustees that the shareholders so voting were the owners of shares to the requisite number at the time of such meeting, the Trustees shall, if the vote is in favor of termination, proceed to wind up this Trust in accordance with Article IX, and in case such vote is in favor of alterations and amendments, shall embody the same in a Supplementary Declaration of Trust which they shall sign and deliver to said Old Colony Trust Company, and which, being so executed and delivered, shall be conclusive evidence of the due adoption by the shareholders of the alterations and amendments therein contained and thereafter shall have the same operation and effect as if originally embodied in this instrument.

In Witness WHEREOF the said Trustees above named have hereunto set their hands this 20th day of January, 1914.
Cranmore N. Wallace
Ernest W. Bowditch
Charles W. Hubbard
Edwin F. Greene

TRUSTEES: Robert F. Herrick
Emor H. Harding
Henry O. Underwood
Sidney Stevens

COMMONWEALTH OF MASSACHUSETTS.
SUFFOLK, SS.

THE SOUTHERN BRICK WORKS

INDENTURE OF TRUST

This INDENTURE made at........ in the County of........ and State of........ the........ day of June 1920 by and between ee eeeee (hereinafter called the Grantor) of the one part and seeeeee and........all of the said........(hereinafter called the Trustees, which expression shall extend to and include the trustees for the time being of these presents and the word "Trustee" shall apply to any one of the Trustees where the context so admits) of the other part.

Whereas the Grantor is the owner of certain lands and other property hereinafter described and desires to convey the same to the Trustees upon the trusts and for the purposes hereinafter set forth and declared.

Now, THEREFORE, in consideration of the premises and of the mutual promises herein contained and of the sum of one dollar and other good and valuable consideration to it in hand paid by the Trustees receipt whereof is hereby acknowledged the Grantor does hereby convey and warrant unto the Trustees their heirs and assigns forever not in tenancy in common but in joint tenancy in fee simple the following described property situated in the city of se eeeees County of......... Together with all of the buildings machinery tools equipment materials and other property thereon and all rights claims choses in action bills receivable moneys and other personal property tangible or intangible now owned by the Grantor subject however to its outstanding debts and obligations.

To have and to hold all and singular the said property unto and to the use of the Trustees their heirs and assigns forever not in tenancy in common but in joint tenancy but in trust nevertheless to sell and convert the same into cash or personal property, and in the meantime to manage the same and to receive the income thereof for the benefit of the holders for the time being of the shares hereinafter mentioned (hereinafter called the Shareholders) according to the number and kind of shares held by them respectively and with and subject to the powers and provisions hereinafter contained concerning the same. (All of the said property hereinbefore mentioned or described, whether real or personal, and all property of whatever kind or nature that may hereafter become subject to the trusts of this indenture or be transferred to or vested

<br>

### --- Page 0602 ---

THE SOUTHERN BRICK WORKS

in the Trustees is generally hereinafter referred to as the "Trust Premises").

The TRUSTEES

1. All the property and effects for the time being subject to any of the trusts of these presents shall be transferred to and vested in the Trustees as joint tenants.

2. The Trustees shall have power subject to the limitations and conditions of these presents from time to time:

(a) To sell and convert the real estate for such price or consideration payable at such time or times or otherwise as they may determine, and to convey the same, but so that they may postpone such sale and conversion so long as they in their uncontrolled discretion shall think proper, without being responsible for loss, and meanwhile for the purpose of promoting such sales to improve in any way and develop the said real estate or any part thereof and to erect, pull down, improve, and repair buildings thereon.

(b) To transfer all or any part of the trust premises to any corporation, person, or persons upon such terms, trusts, and conditions as the Trustees may determine, and to grant leases of the whole or any part or parts thereof that shall for the time being remain unsold for any term of years, beyond the possible termination of this trust or for any less term, in possession or reversion or at will and with or without option to purchase, and to make allowances to and arrangements with tenants and others, and to accept surrenders of leases and tenancies, and to manage the trust premises, and to make such outlay as the Trustees shall consider proper for the said purposes or for the benefit of the said real estate.

(c) To manage the trust premises and to conduct thereon the business of manufacturing, building and other brick and clay products and to sell, deal in and otherwise dispose of the same and to acquire by purchase or otherwise all materials, tools, machinery, plant, buildings, equipment and appliances necessary for that purpose or deemed by them to be of advantage in carrying on the business of the trust.

(d) To acquire any real or personal property, rights, franchises or privileges which they may think suitable or convenient for any purpose of the undertaking.

(e) To borrow, raise or secure the payment of money for the purposes of the said trusts by mortgage, pledge, or charge of the whole or any part of the trust premises, and to give proper securities or evidences for the moneys so raised, which may mature at a time or times beyond the possible termination of this trust or at any earlier time or times.

<br>

### --- Page 0603 ---

(f) To insure the buildings or other property for the time being subject to any of the said trusts or the rents or rental value thereof against loss or damage by fire or other casualty and to apply the moneys received under any insurance on any such buildings in rebuilding, repairing, or replacing the same or otherwise as may seem most advantageous, and to insure against injury to person or property during construction or any other time.

(g) To employ such servants or agents as they shall think proper for the sale or management of any of the said property or in conducting the business of the said trust without the Trustees being answerable for the acts and defaults of such agents.

(h) To settle all accounts, and to compound, compromise, abandon, or adjust by arbitration or otherwise any actions, suits, proceedings, disputes, claims, demands, and things relating to the trust premises, and to give time with or without security for the payment or delivery of any debts or property, and to execute and enter into releases, agreements, and other instruments, and do all other things proper for any such purpose and to pay or satisfy any debts or claims upon any evidence that they shall think sufficient.

(i) To enter into arrangements expressed in writing for any of the purposes hereinbefore mentioned, that the payment of any sum of money or the performance of anything that shall have been agreed upon shall be a floating charge upon the said undertaking and the real and personal property for the time being subject to the trusts of these presents in such manner that the Trustees notwithstanding such charge may continue to carry on the said trusts and deal with the said real and personal property by way of sale, mortgage, charge, lease, or otherwise or making distributions thereout in accordance with the trusts hereof, and that the Trustees shall not be liable for the payment of any such sum of money or for damages for non-performance of anything that shall have been agreed upon except out of the trust premises so far as the same shall be sufficient for the purpose, and that no Trustee shall be in any way liable in respect thereof after he ceases to be a Trustee of these presents, and every such floating charge shall rank part passu with every such other floating charge unless otherwise specially agreed. And any agreement entered into in pursuance of these presents in the name of Southern Brick Works shall without any express provisions to that effect operate as an arrangement with the Trustees for such a floating charge as above mentioned, and words may be added to any document containing or relating to any such agreement or any part thereof to the effect that contracts made in that name operate as a floating charge created by the Trustees upon all the property comprised in these

<br>

### --- Page 0604 ---

THE SOUTHERN BRICK WORKS

presents as herein provided and that the Trustees are not liable upon any such contract except for the application of such property in accordance with the provisions hereof and that no liability attaches to the Shareholders.

(j) Generally in all matters to deal with the trust premises, and to manage and control the same and conduct the said trust as fully as if the Trustees were the absolute owners of the trust premises, and to execute and do all such agreements, deeds, instruments and things as the Trustees may deem proper for any of the said purposes.

But the Trustees shall not have any power or authority to borrow money on the credit or on behalf of the Shareholders personally or to make any contract binding the Shareholders personally for repayment of any money raised by mortgage, pledge, or charge in pursuance of the provisions hereof, or to make any contract or incur any liability whatever on behalf of the Shareholders binding them personally, and each contract shall contain substantially the following clause in the body thereof: “This contract is made for and on behalf of the Southern Brick Works, a trust established by an Indenture of Trust dated June Ist, 1920, and recorded in the office of the Register of Deeds of........ County, and imposes no personal liability upon the Trustees or Shareholders of the said trust.”

3. The said undertaking and all business relating thereto may be carried on by the Trustees under the name of Southern Brick Works at such place or places as they shall from time to time appoint.

4. All the real estate comprised in these presents or at any time hereafter acquired by or conveyed to the Trustees for any of the purposes of the said trusts shall be held by the Trustees or Trustee for the time being of these presents and their heirs and assigns upon trust to sell or convert the same with all the powers in that behalf of an absolute owner and out of the proceeds of such sale or conversion to pay the expenses incurred in such sale or conversion and to hold the rest of the said proceeds and the net rents and profits of the said real estate until the same shall be sold upon the trusts and subject to the powers and provisions concerning the same contained in this indenture.

5. Out of the receipts from the trust premises the Trustees may make distributions among the Shareholders according to the number and kind of shares held by them respectively at such times as the Trustees may determine, and the Shareholders shall have no right to any distributions except when and as declared by the Trustees.

6. The Trustees shall be entitled to receive reasonable remu-

<br>

### --- Page 0605 ---

APPENDIX OF FORMS

Remuneration for the services rendered by them in connection with the trusts hereof.

7. Every Trustee may purchase or acquire shares in all respects as if he were not a Trustee.

8. Of the said Trustees........ shall hold office for the term of six years from date hereof,........ shall hold office for the term of four years from the said date and........ shall hold office for the term of two years from the said date. Upon the expiration of the term of office of any Trustee his successor shall be appointed as hereinafter provided for the term of six years.

9. Any Trustee may retire by presenting his resignation in writing to some one or more of the other Trustees, but such resignation shall be effectual and complete only upon the appointment of a new Trustee in his place, or the previous acceptance of his resignation by the remaining Trustees or upon the expiration of six calendar months after such resignation, and meanwhile he shall continue to act as such Trustee.

10. The Trustees shall have power at any time or times to accept the resignation of any Trustee and with the consent in writing of the holders of two-thirds of the shares then outstanding to remove any Trustee or to appoint any new Trustee or Trustees.

11. Upon the death, resignation or removal of any Trustee the continuing Trustee or Trustees shall continue to exercise and discharge all of the powers, discretion, and duties hereby imposed or conferred upon the Trustees, and upon every appointment of a new Trustee a certificate signed and acknowledged by the Trustees or a majority of them reciting such death, resignation or removal and such consent and containing the appointment of a new Trustee or Trustees and recorded in the office of the Register of Deeds in the County of... .and State of........ shall vest the title to the trust premises in the Trustees for the time being.

12. The receipts of the Trustees or either or any of them for moneys or things paid or delivered to them or him shall be effectual discharges to the persons paying or delivering the same therefrom and no person from whom the Trustees shall receive any money, property or other credit shall be required to see to the application thereof. And no purchaser or person dealing with the Trustees shall be bound to ascertain or inquire as to the existence or occurrence of any event or purpose in or for which a sale, mortgage, pledge, or charge is herein authorized or directed or otherwise as to the purpose or regularity of any of the acts of the Trustees, or any of them, purporting to be done in pursuance of any of the provisions or powers herein contained or as to the regularity of the discharge, resignation, or appointment of any Trustee.

<br>

### --- Page 0606 ---

THE SOUTHERN BRICK WORKS

Trustee. And no purchaser or person dealing with any Trustee purporting to act during the absence of any other Trustee or under any delegation of authority from any other Trustee shall be concerned to ascertain or inquire whether an occasion exists in which he is authorized so to act or in which such delegation is permitted or whether such delegated authority is still subsisting. 13. The Trustees shall not be liable for anything done or omitted by them in good faith, and shall be answerable and accountable only for their own acts, receipts, neglects, and defaults respectively and not for those of each other or any servant or agent employed by them, nor for any bank, trust company, broker, or other person with whom or into whose hands any moneys or securities may be deposited or come, nor for any defect in title of any property acquired, nor for any loss unless it shall happen through their own default respectively, and they shall be entitled to indemnity, out of the trust premises against any liability incurred in the execution of the terms or provisions hereof. And no Trustee, however appointed, shall be obliged to give any bond or surety or other security for the performance of any of his duties in the said trusts.

14. The Trustees may act with or without a meeting, and by proxy, and the action of a majority of the Trustees shall be valid and binding as the action of the Trustees and all leases, contracts, and other documents, except conveyances of lands, executed by any two of the Trustees hereunder shall be valid and as effectual and binding as if executed by all the Trustees. And any Trustee may by power of attorney empower any other Trustee to execute in his name and on his behalf any and all documents, including conveyances of lands, for any of the purposes of these presents without being responsible for loss. The Trustees shall keep such records of their proceedings as they may deem advisable.

DURATION OF THE TRUSTS

15. The trusts contained in these presents shall continue in such manner that the Trustees shall have all the powers and discretions expressed to be given to them respectively by these presents, and that no Shareholder shall be entitled to put an end to the same or to require a division of the trust premises of any part thereof until the expiration of 21 years from the death of the last survivor of the said........ and........ respectively and such other persons now living as shall hereafter become trustees of these presents before any person or persons not now living shall have become the only Trustee or Trustees thereof or the offices of Trustees shall be wholly vacant or until the whole of the trust premises shall have been converted into money or transferred to a corporation or to another trust in exchange for shares or securities

<br>

### --- Page 0607 ---

APPENDIX OF FORMS

of such corporation or trust, whichever of the said events shall first occur.

16. Upon the termination of the said trusts by the said limitation the Trustees shall sell and convert into money the whole of the trust premises then remaining in their hands and shall apportion the net proceeds thereof among all the Shareholders ratably according to the number and kind of the said shares held by them respectively, except, that in the event of a termination by reason of a transfer of all of the trust premises to a corporation or trust as aforesaid, the Trustees may in their discretion apportion any or all of the shares or securities of such corporation or trust, so received, among all the Shareholders ratably according to the number and kind of the said shares held by them respectively. And in making any sale under this provision the Trustees shall have power to sell by public auction or private contract, and to buy in or rescind or vary any contract of sale, and to resell without being answerable for loss, and for the said purposes to do and execute all proper deeds, instruments, and things.

INDEMNITY OF TRUSTEES

17. The Trustees shall be entitled to indemnity out of the trust premises against any and all liability incurred by them or any of them in respect of the premises. But the Trustees or any of them shall not be entitled to look to the Shareholders personally for indemnity against any liability incurred by them or any of them in the execution hereof or to call upon the Shareholders for the payment of any sum of money or any assessment whatever.

SHARES AND SHAREHOLDERS

18. The Trustees shall issue shares representing the beneficial interest of the Shareholders in the trust premises, to or upon the order of the Grantor. Such shares shall be of an aggregate number of not exceeding........ shares having no par value.

19. New shares in addition to those above mentioned may be issued by the Trustees from time to time thereafter as the needs of the trust may in their judgment require, for such price or other consideration as the Trustees may deem proper.

20. Such new shares may in the discretion of the Trustees be issued as preferred or common shares ranking pari passu with any of the preferred or common shares previously issued or with any right of preference or with any other special privilege or advantage or with any deferred rights as compared with any shares previously issued or then about to be issued.

21. The shares issued by the Trustees hereunder or the proceeds thereof may be applied by them to the payment or discharge

<br>

### --- Page 0608 ---

THE SOUTHERN BRICK WORKS

of any expenses or obligations incurred or assumed by them in connection with the acquisition of the trust premises or the improvement or development thereof, or to any other objects deemed by them to be beneficial to the trust.

22. A register or registers shall be kept by or under the direction of the Trustees which shall contain the names and addresses of the Shareholders and the number and kind of shares held by them respectively and all future transfers thereof. No Shareholder shall be entitled to have any notice given to him as herein provided until he has given his address to the Trustees to be entered in the register.

23. The Trustees shall have power (but shall not be required) to employ some incorporated bank or trust company as transfer agent or registrar to keep the said registers, and to record therein the transfers of any of the shares, and to register certificates of shares issued by the Trustees to the persons entitled to the same after any transfers of such shares or otherwise. And the remuneration of such transfer agent or registrar shall be allowed as part of the expenses incidental to the execution of the said trusts.

24. Every Shareholder shall be entitled to receive from the Trustees a certificate signed by at least two Trustees in such form as the Trustees may approve, specifying the number and kind of shares held by him, and every certificate of shares shall be registered in the said register or registers.

25. Shares shall be personal property entitling the Shareholders only to the rights and interests in the trust premises set forth in these presents.

26. Every transfer of any share (otherwise than by operation of law) shall be in writing under the hand of the transferor and, upon delivery thereof with the existing certificate for such share to the Trustees, shall be recorded in the register, and a new certificate therefor shall be given to the transferee, and in case of a transfer of only a part of the shares mentioned in any certificate a new certificate for the residue thereof shall be given to the transferor. Until the transfer shall be so delivered and recorded, the transferor shall be deemed to be the holder of the share or shares comprised therein for all the purposes hereof, and the Trustees shall not be affected by any notice of the transfer.

27. Any person becoming entitled to any share in consequence of the death, bankruptcy, or insolvency of any Shareholder or in any way other than by a transfer in accordance with the preceding paragraph shall be recorded in the register as the holder of the said share and receive a new certificate for the same upon production of the proper evidence thereof and delivery of the existing certificate to the Trustees or their transfer agent or registrar.

<br>

### --- Page 0609 ---

APPENDIX OF FORMS

28. Two or more persons holding any share shall be joint tenants of the entire interest therein, and no entry shall be made in the register or in any certificate that any person is entitled to any future, limited, or contingent interest in any share. But any person registered as the holder of any share may, subject to the provisions hereinafter contained, be described therein as a trustee of any kind, and any words may be added to the description to identify the trust.

29. The Trustees shall not, nor shall the Shareholders or any transfer agent or registrar or other agent of the Trustees be bound to take notice or be affected by notice of any trust whether express, implied, or constructive, or any charge or equity to which any of the said shares or the interests of any of the Shareholders in the trusts of these presents may be subject, or to ascertain or inquire whether any sale or transfer of any such share or interest by any such Shareholder or his personal representatives is authorized by such trust, charge, or equity, or to recognize any person as having any interest therein except the persons registered as such Shareholders. And the receipt of the person in whose name any share is registered or, if such share is registered in the names of more than one person, the receipt of any one of such persons shall be a sufficient discharge for all distributions and other moneys payable in respect of such share and from all liability to see to the application thereof.

30. Every notice to the Shareholders required or provided for in these presents may be given to them personally or by sending — it to them through the post-office in a prepaid letter addressed to each of them respectively at his address specified in the register and shall be deemed to have been given at the time when it is posted. But in respect of any share held jointly by several persons notice so given to whichever of them is first named in the register shall be sufficient notice to all of them. And any notice so sent to the registered address of any Shareholder shall be deemed to have been duly sent in respect of any such share whether held by him solely or jointly with others, notwithstanding he be then deceased and whether the Trustees or any person sending such notice have knowledge or not of his death until some other person or persons shall be registered as holders. And the certificate of the person or persons giving such notice shall be sufficient evidence thereof and shall protect all persons acting in good faith in reliance on such certificate. Any notice herein required to be given by advertisement shall be given by publishing the same once in one daily newspaper published at........ and in such other newspapers, if any, as the person or persons giving such notice shall think proper.

MISCELLANEOUS PROVISIONS

31. The trust provided for in these presents may be designated for all the purposes thereof as Southern Brick Works and under that name so far as practicable all business may be conducted by the Trustees. The Trustees may be referred to as the Trustees of Southern Brick Works.

32. In the construction of these presents words in the singular number include the plural number and vice versa, and words denoting males include females, and words denoting persons include firms, associations, and corporations unless a contrary intention is to be inferred from the subject-matter or context. All the trusts, powers, and provisions herein contained shall take effect and be construed according to the law of Massachusetts.

33. The terms and provisions of these presents may be amended or changed in any manner or to any extent by a majority of the Trustees, but such amendment or change before becoming effective shall require the concurrence in writing of the holders of three-fourths of all the shares then outstanding but not so as to affect the validity or effect of anything previously done by the Trustees hereunder. (Construed in Crehan v. Megargel, 234 N. Y. 67, 136 N. E. 296.)
This INDENTURE, made this first day of June, 1915, between the
various parties whose names are subscribed hereto, hereinafter
called the "Subscribers," parties of the first part, and Ralph G.
Megargel as Trustee, party of the second part,
WITNESSETH:

The Subscribers, in consideration of the covenants and agree-
ments hereinafter set forth and of the sum of one dollar to each of
them in hand paid by the party of the second part, severally promise
and agree, for themselves and their respective executors and
administrators, to pay to the party of the second part in cash the
sums (which shall be some multiple of one thousand dollars) set
opposite their respective signatures on demand at any time prior
to the 5th day of January, 1916, to have and to hold the same in
trust, nevertheless, for the equal and pro rata benefit of the
Subscribers, their respective executors, administrators and assigns,
upon the terms, covenants and conditions and upon the uses and
trusts and for the purposes hereinafter set forth: Namely:

First.—To invest the aggregate of said sums, collect and
receive the interest, income and profits thereof and pay the same
over, as hereinafter provided, to the Subscribers, their respective
executors, administrators or assigns, ratably in proportion to their
respective subscriptions, and upon the termination of the trust
hereby created, to pay over the said aggregate principal sum, or
so much thereof as may then remain, in like manner.

Second.—The Trustee shall invest the said aggregate principal
sum by contributing the same, and all thereof, in his own name and
as his sole and individual special capital to a limited or special
partnership to be formed under the Laws of the State of New York
for the purposes and upon the terms and conditions set forth in
the proposed Articles of Partnership hereto annexed (such part-
nership being hereinafter referred to as the "said partnership").

Third.—The aggregate sum to be invested and contributed as
special capital as aforesaid shall be subject in all respects to the
debts of said partnership, and it, and the party hereto of the
second part as special partner, shall be subject in all respects to
the other terms and provisions of the said Articles and to the
Statute of the State of New York governing limited or special

<br>

### --- Page 0613 ---

APPENDIX OF FORMS

partnerships, and the Subscribers, or their executors, administrators or assigns, shall have no right of accounting or other rights whatsoever, against the said partnership, but shall, in all respects, be strangers thereto and, as regards the trust property and estate or any of the rights and interests guaranteed them hereby, shall look only to the party of the second part or his legal representatives; excepting only, in the event of the dissolution of the said partnership (or of any successor partnership that may be formed as hereinafter provided) caused by death of the party hereto of the second part and the consequent termination of the trust hereby created, to receive from the survivors of the said partnership, or of such successor partnership, the amount of the said special capital that may remain after the final liquidation of the business thereof. In any such event the said amount shall be paid over to the Metropolitan Trust Company hereinafter named for distribution among the Subscribers as hereinafter provided.

Fourth.—The party of the second part shall, upon payment to him of the respective amounts subscribed hereunder, issue receipts to the several Subscribers, in the form hereto annexed marked “A,” for the amounts of their respective payments. Such receipts shall be non-negotiable but shall be assignable, but no assignment thereof shall be valid unless made on books to be kept for the purpose as hereinafter provided.

Fifth.—The party of the second part shall cause to be kept at the office of Metropolitan Trust Company, in the City of New York, books in which the names of the holders of the said receipts, and their respective addresses shall be registered, and upon the presentation of any receipt a new receipt, or new receipts aggregating the principal amount represented by the receipt so presented, shall, upon the surrender and cancellation of the old receipt, be issued to such person or persons as the registered holder of the old receipt, or his attorney duly authorized in writing, may direct; provided that no receipt shall be issued except for an amount in some multiple of one thousand dollars.

Sixth.—The party of the second part, upon the receipt by him of any interest or profits to which he may be entitled as special partner as aforesaid, shall forthwith deposit, or cause to be deposited, the same in the said Metropolitan Trust Company and the same shall forthwith be distributed by it among the then registered holders of the said receipts in the proportions that the several amounts represented thereby bear to the total amount thereof from time to time outstanding. Such payments shall be made by checks mailed to the holders, of the said receipts, as the same appear upon the books to be kept as aforesaid, and to the addresses appearing thereon, and it is expressly understood and agreed that

<br>

### --- Page 0614 ---

BUSINESS TRUST AND LIMITED PARTNERSHIP

the Trust Company shall be under no liability whatsoever, nor incur any obligation whatsoever, nor be under any duty whatsoever, except only to receive whatever sums may be paid to it, or caused to be paid to it, by the party of the second part (or by any special partner succeeding him as hereinafter provided), and pay out the same as aforesaid; and it especially shall be under no obligation or duty whatsoever to make any inquiry as to the correctness of the amounts so paid or cause to be paid to it or as to the title of the registered holder of any receipt.

Seventh.—Upon the receipt by the party of the second part, as special partner aforesaid, of the audited annual statements to be furnished the several partners as in the said partnership Articles provided, he shall forthwith mail a copy thereof to each of the holders of the receipts issued hereunder at the respective addresses appearing upon the books to be kept as aforesaid.

Eighth.—The party of the second part further covenants and agrees (a) to make, or cause to be made, from time to time, all such examinations of the books, accounts and business of the said partnership as he may deem advisable and as he may be authorized to make as special partner and to report the result thereof to the Subscribers, (b) to endorse and protect his rights and interests as such special partner in all respects as if the Subscribers had no beneficial interest in the money paid by them hereunder and to that end to bring, in his own name and as having the sole legal title to the special capital contributed by him, all such actions or proceedings, whether for an accounting or otherwise, as he may deem necessary or desirable, and (c) to faithfully, fully and duly observe the Statute of the State of New York governing special or limited partnerships and the duties and obligations of special partners.

Ninth.—The party of the second part shall be authorized to deduct all costs and expenses that may be incurred by him in enforcing and protecting his rights as a special partner.

Tenth.—Neither the party of the second part, nor the other members of the said partnership, shall be under any duty, liability or responsibility whatsoever to the Subscribers for any of the acts, investments, purchases, losses or business transactions of the partnership nor shall the party of the second part be liable or responsible in any manner whatsoever to them except for his own wilful negligence or default.

Eleventh.—The party of the second part shall be under no obligation to commence any action or legal proceedings to protect his rights and interests as special partner unless requested so to do in writing by fifty-one per centum in amount of the Subscribers, and unless indemnified against all liability or expense he may incur thereby. In case such request be made the remain-

<br>

### --- Page 0615 ---

Subscribers shall be bound thereby and the indemnity above provided for shall not be resorted to until after all the interests and profits and (if such action or proceeding be in respect of his liquidating rights) all the special capital belonging to the party of the second part as special partner has first been exhausted.

TWELFTH.—Upon the dissolution of the said partnership, whether in accordance with its terms or otherwise, or upon the dissolution of any successor partnership that may be formed within the period of five years as hereinafter provided, the party of the second part shall forthwith, upon the surrender of the receipts severally held by them, pay over, or cause to be paid over, to the Subscribers, in the proportions to which they are severally entitled, the amount of special capital together with any interest and profits, coming to him upon the final liquidation of the said partnership or of such successor partnership; less any deductions chargeable against the same as hereinbefore provided. The payment to the Subscribers may be made to the said Trust Company for their account.

THIRTEENTH.—In the event of the dissolution of the said partnership caused by death of any partner, other than the party hereto of the second part, the party hereto of the second part shall, provided the surviving partners so agree, enter into a new limited or special partnership with the survivors and shall contribute as special capital thereto the amount of special capital (including any undistributed interest and profits accrued to the date of dissolution) he may be entitled to receive upon the liquidation of the old partnership. The provisions of this section shall apply to successive dissolutions caused by death of any partner, other than the party hereto of the second part, prior to the expiration of five years from the date of the commencement of the partnership to be formed by the annexed articles.

FOURTEENTH.—In the event of the dissolution of the said partnership caused by death of the party of the second part, the Subscribers agree with each other that, with the consent of the surviving partners, a new limited or special partnership shall be formed with a special partner to be designated by an appointment in writing to be signed by not less than fifty-one per centum in amount of the subscribers and that the amount of special capital (including any undistributed interest and profits accrued to the date of dissolution) which, as beneficiaries of the trust hereby created, they may be severally entitled to receive upon the liquidation of the old firm shall be paid over by them, or caused to be paid, to such person and the same shall be contributed by him as special capital to the new partnership. The provisions of this section shall apply to successive dissolutions caused by death of the

<br>

### --- Page 0616 ---

special partner prior to the expiration of the five year period above mentioned.

Fifteenth.—It is expressly understood and agreed, anything herein contained to the contrary notwithstanding, that, with the consent of all the general partners of the said partnership or of any successor partnership, an additional general partner or additional general partners may at any time be admitted into the partnership.

Sixteenth.—In the event of the formation of any new limited or special partnership (whether such new partnership be one succeeding upon dissolution caused by death as aforesaid or one formed upon the admission of a general partner or partners as required by law) the same shall, in its formation and otherwise, duly and fully comply with all the provisions of the Statute of the State of New York in such case made and provided. The Articles of any such partnership shall be in all respects identical with the annexed Articles except only in the following respects, namely: (a) any reduction in the amount of special capital to be contributed by the party hereto of the second part (or the special partner succeeding him in any successor partnership as aforesaid) resulting from loss in the amount of capital contributed to the old partnership; (b) the term of the partnership which shall not, however, extend beyond the expiration of the five year period above mentioned, (c) in the event of the addition of any new general partner, either before or after dissolution caused by death as aforesaid, the insertion of his name and of the amount, if any, of general capital to be contributed by him; (d) any readjustment of the interest in the profits of the general partners, whether new or original, and of their liability for losses, that may be agreed upon; (e) such changes in the date of determining annual profits, or in other dates, as may be necessary; and (f) any provisions that may be agreed upon, and approved by a Certified Public Accountant as provided in the annexed Articles covering the contribution as general capital of the respective interests of the general partners in the old partnership, the adjustment of the accounts of the old partnership, and the assumption, if any, of the debts, liabilities and contracts of the old partnership.

It is, however, expressly understood and agreed that (a) the proportion of profits accruing to the special partner, whether he be the party hereto of the second part or a special partner succeeding him and his liability for losses, as provided in the annexed Articles, and the limitation of his losses to the amount of his special capital also as therein provided, shall in any event, and in any and every case, remain wholly unchanged and he shall be entitled to such percentage of profits, and be only liable for

<br>

### --- Page 0617 ---

APPENDIX OF FORMS

losses, irrespective of any increase in the number of general partners or of any additional contributions of general capital or of any reduction in the amount of special capital contributed resulting from loss as aforesaid and that (b) in the event of dissolution, the provisions of the Statute covering the transaction of business by the old partnership after dissolution shall be fully and duly observed.

SEVENTEENTH.—In the event of the formation of any new limited or special partnership pursuant to either of the foregoing provisions the special partner thereof, and of any successor thereof, shall be subject in all respects to the obligations, and entitled to the rights and privileges; herein set forth, and the Subscribers shall likewise be so subject and entitled, and this Indenture, and all and every the provisions thereof, shall be read and construed as if any such new or successor partnership, and the Articles thereof, had originally, and alone, been mentioned herein. In the event of the death of the party of the second part, any special partner of any and all new limited or special partnerships that may be formed as above provided shall, upon his appointment, endorse hereon his acceptance of all the trusts and provisions hereof and shall be a party hereto as if originally, and alone, named as the party hereto of the second part.

EIGHTEENTH.—This Indenture shall not be binding on any of the parties hereto until five hundred thousand ($500,000) dollars have been subscribed hereunder. It is understood and agreed that, after the formation of the limited or special partnership under the annexed articles and the original contribution of special capital by the party of the second part, this Indenture may be signed by additional Subscribers and that, the party of the second part, as special partner, shall from time to time upon the payment of such additional subscriptions increase the special capital originally contributed by him to the said partnership (or to any successor partnership) in the manner, and subject to the provisions, set forth in the Statute of the State of New York in such case made and provided. In such case the additional Subscribers shall (subject to the provisions hereinafter set forth) be subject in all respects to, and entitled to the benefits of, the provisions of this Indenture and the same shall be read and construed as if the original Subscribers and such additional Subscribers had signed at one and the same time; provided, however, that no addition of special capital shall be made at any time in a less amount than one hundred thousand ($100,000) dollars, and that, from and after the formation of the said partnership, any additional Subscribers shall not have any rights whatsoever hereunder, or be deemed parties hereto, until their subscriptions from time to time aggregate said

<br>

### --- Page 0618 ---

BUSINESS TRUST AND LIMITED PARTNERSHIP

sum of one hundred thousand ($100,000) dollars nor until the same are paid to the party of the second part and receipted for by him as above provided; and further provided, that the special capital shall not be increased by additional subscriptions to an amount in excess of one million ($1,000,000) dollars, except with the consent of the general partners and of the holders of receipts representing two-thirds in amount of the special capital at the time subscribed and paid in.

In the event of any such subsequent contributions of additional special capital by the special partner (either to the said partnership or to any successor partnership) the proportion of total profits accruing to special capital as provided in the annexed Articles (to wit, thirty-three and one-third (33 1/3) per centum thereof) shall remain unchanged and all Subscribers, whether original or additional, shall share pro rata hereunder in the proportions that the amounts of their respective subscriptions bear to the total amount at any time subscribed hereunder.

It is expressly understood and agreed that, except as hereinafter expressly provided, there shall be no differentiation whatsoever between original and subsequent contributions of special capital, either on the books of the said partnership, or of any successor partnership, or as between the Subscribers hereunder, and that all contributions, whenever made, shall stand upon the same pro rata footing and be treated in all respects as if made at one and the same time, without any preference, discrimination or priority, one contribution over the other, in respect of sharing in profits or losses, making good any impairment of special capital (whether the same existed at the time of making any additional contributions of special capital or arose subsequent thereto) or otherwise howsoever; provided, however, that before any additional special capital shall be contributed the partnership books and accounts shall be audited and adjusted, in the manner provided in the tenth and eleventh sections of the annexed Articles, and the interest and profits, if any, actually accrued on the special capital previously contributed shall, as fixed and determined by such audit and adjustment, be credited to a separate account on the partnership books, subject to the debts and losses of the partnership and to the provisions of the Statute of the State of New York governing payment of interest and profits to special partners, shall be paid over to the special partner and distributed by him in the manner above provided only among the previous Subscribers hereunder.

The receipts to be given any additional Subscribers shall be identical with the annexed form, except that they shall contain an appropriate provision to the effect that the holder thereof shall not be entitled to share in the accrued interest and profits to

<br>

### --- Page 0619 ---

APPENDIX OF FORMS

which, as above provided, the special capital previously contributed may be entitled and to that end they shall be differentiated from receipts previously issued, by appropriate serial numbering and lettering.

NINETEENTH.—The adjustments of accounts provided for in the foregoing section and all adjustments of the books and accounts of the said partnership, or of any successor partnership, made for the purpose of ascertaining annual interest or profits or otherwise, shall, when made and audited as provided in the annexed Articles, be absolutely final and conclusive upon all persons now or hereafter parties to this Indenture, and when so made and audited, the party of the second part shall not be under any duty, liability or responsibility whatsoever to the Subscribers, or any of them, in respect thereof or as to the correctness thereof or of the amounts of interest, profits and losses fixed and ascertained thereby.

TWENTIETH.—The term Subscribers, wherever herein used, means the registered holders from time to time of the receipts issued by the party of the second part hereunder and the executors, administrators and legal representatives of such holders.

TWENTY-FIRST.—This Indenture is binding upon such executors, administrators and legal representatives and upon the executors, administrators and legal representatives of the party of the second part.

TWENTY-SECOND.—This Indenture may be executed by the Trustee and the Subscribers in duplicate, and the several duplicates shall constitute one and the same instrument.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the party of the second part has hereunto subscribed his name and affixed his seal, and the parties of the first part have subscribed their several names and placed opposite thereto the respective amounts agreed to be paid by them hereunder, the day and year first above written.

(Sgd) RALPH G. MEGARGEL.
Witness C. W. Byram.

Name of Subscribers Addresses Amounts: "A"

RECEIPT ISSUED UNDER A CERTAIN INDENTURE, dated the Ist day of June, 1915, executed and delivered between the Subscribers, parties thereto of the first part, and RALPH G. MEGARGEL, as Trustee, party thereto of the second part; the original of which said Indenture is on file in the office of the Metropolitan Trust Company, of the City of New York.

<br>

### --- Page 0620 ---

BUSINESS TRUST AND LIMITED PARTNERSHIP

No......... $........
It Is HEREBY CERTIFIED that this receipt represents the principal sum of $......... paid to me, as Trustee, under the above mentioned Indenture.

This receipt, and the registered holder thereof, are subject to all and every the terms and provisions, and entitled to all and every the benefits, of the said Indenture.

The registered holder hereof is entitled to share in all payments made under the said Indenture in the proportion that the principal sum represented hereby bears to the total aggregate amount paid by the parties of the first part to the said Indenture.

This receipt is non-negotiable and is transferable by the registered holder, or his agent duly authorized in writing, only upon books kept for the purpose at the office of the Trust Company above mentioned.

Upon the surrender and cancellation hereof new receipts may, as endorsed hereon by the registered holder or his agent as aforesaid, be issued in substitution hereof to the aggregate amount of the principal sum represented hereby; provided, however, that no receipt shall be issued except for an amount in some multiple of one thousand dollars.

In witness whereof I, the party of the second part to the Indenture above mentioned, have subscribed my name this.......... day of.......... 1915.

(FORM FOR REGISTRATION)

Date of Registration Name of Registered Owner Signature of Registrar

For VALUE RECEIVED,........ hereby sell, assign and transfer unto........ all the property, rights and interests represented by the within Receipt, and hereby constitute and appoint........ true and lawful attorney, irrevocable, to assign, to transfer, and to execute all necessary acts of assignment or transfer of, all said property, rights and interests upon the books to be kept at the Trust Company mentioned within.

ARTICLES OF PARTNERSHIP REFERRED TO IN THE FOREGOING INDENTURE.

AGREEMENT, entered into this first day of June, 1915, Between Roy C. Mecarcet, Frank B. Erwin, Ciarence W. Byram and Cuaruzs N. Jones, parties of the first part, and Ratpx G. MzGARGEL, party of the second part.

First.—The parties hereto have agreed, and do hereby agree,

<br>

### --- Page 0621 ---

APPENDIX OF FORMS

to form a limited or special partnership for the purpose of carrying on a general commission, brokerage, bond, investment and mercantile business in the City of New York, and in such other place or places as may be hereafter agreed upon.

Second.—The business of the partnership shall be conducted under the firm name and style of "Megargel & Company."

Third.—The partnership shall commence on the first day of September, 1915, and shall terminate on the expiration of five years from said date.

Fourth.—The said Ralph G. Megargel shall be a special partner and, as such, shall contribute as special capital to the partnership the sum of Ten hundred thousand ($1,000,000) dollars in cash, payable on or before the said 5th day of January, 1916. The said special partner shall, subject to the provisions of the Statute of the State of New York governing limited or special partnerships, receive interest at the rate of six per centum per annum upon the capital contributed by him and be interested in and entitled to receive thirty-three and one-third (33 1/3) per centum of the profits of the partnership.

Fifth.—The said Roy C. Megargel, Frank B. Erwin, Clarence W. Byram, and Charles N. Jones shall be general partners and shall contribute to the general capital the assets of the present firm of Megargel and Company which assets, for the purpose of the allowance of interest thereon, as hereinafter provided, shall be deemed to be of the fixed value of five hundred thousand ($500,000) dollars.

Sixth.—The general partners shall be entitled to and interested in the profits of the partnership in the following proportions, namely: The said Roy C. Megargel........ (cee.) per centum thereof; Frank B. Erwin........ ) per centum thereof; Clarence W. Byram........ (......) per centum thereof; Charles N. Jones.......... (....0-) per centum thereof; and each of the general partners shall be allowed interest at the rate of six per centum per annum upon all balances, including their respective capitals, standing to their credits upon the books of the partnership, and shall be charged interest at a like rate on all balances standing to their debits upon said books.
